# BUILD STATE — HilbertRaum

> **This is the handoff/transport file between build steps and sessions.**
> Read this FIRST at the start of every session. Update it at the END of every phase
> (see "Per-phase ritual" in [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md)).
> It carries: current status, decisions, shared data contracts, next actions, open issues.


_2026-06-30 — **Assistant Markdown renderer switched to Streamdown (streaming-aware) + KaTeX math** (ported from
branch `mkg` commit `c5a1e86` onto `mkg-public`, which had diverged at 0.1.34 and never carried it). Replaced
`react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` with **Streamdown** (`@streamdown/math` + `katex`) in `Transcript.tsx`'s shared
`AssistantMarkdown`. **Reverses the FE-1 "live answer is PLAIN TEXT" decision** (architecture.md §FE-1): the live bubble
now streams Markdown — Streamdown block-memoizes (a flush re-parses only the final block) and `parseIncompleteMarkdown`
closes dangling `**bold**`/fences/links so partial syntax formats cleanly. The bubble became `.msg-content.md`; the `▋`
caret stays a sibling. **Improvement over the original commit:** the `components` object is hoisted to module scope
alongside the plugin objects, so the live bubble's per-flush props references are stable — an inline `components` would
have defeated block memoization. **Config:** rehype pared to `rehype-sanitize` only (`rehype-raw` dropped → model HTML
stays LITERAL TEXT; `rehype-harden` dropped as redundant under the CSP + the http(s) `a` gate); `**bold**` mapped back to
`<strong>` (no Tailwind); KaTeX via `plugins={{ math }}` (block `$$…$$`/`\(…\)`, NOT single `$`), fonts bundled as local
assets (offline, `font-src 'self'`). **KaTeX is a deliberate win for tax/finance/accounting users** — typeset effective
rates, depreciation and apportionment formulae instead of raw `\frac{…}{…}` noise. **Measured** (`scripts/bench-markdown-flush.mjs`):
naive full re-parse per flush is O(n²) (6.8× per-block growth, 14.4 s @ 80 KaTeX blocks); block memoization ALONE is flat
O(n) (0.8×, 67 ms); the shipped config (memo + `parseIncompleteMarkdown`) is super-linear (3.0×, 346 ms) — ~40× faster than
naive, ~4 ms/flush, interactive. Residual = the whole-buffer `parseIncompleteMarkdown` scan; upgrade path documented in
§FE-1 (gate `streaming` off past N blocks) — not needed at current sizes. **As built (re-verified on the 2026-07-06 rebase onto
master):** typecheck clean; production build clean **and KaTeX fonts ship locally** (`out/renderer/assets/KaTeX_*`); full
suite green. (An earlier revision of this entry noted "3 pre-existing platform failures" — the Ubuntu path one was fixed
on master by `280cb39` on 2026-07-01 and none reproduce on the rebased tree.) `TranscriptMemo.test.tsx` rewritten to mock Streamdown (live answer now PARSED, persisted turn
still parses once across flushes); new `tests/unit/assistant-markdown.test.tsx` pins the security invariants (http(s) anchor,
inert `javascript:`, no `<script>`) against the real Streamdown. New RENDERER deps: `streamdown`, `@streamdown/math`,
`katex`; `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` removed. The screenshot-verify harness
(`preview.tsx` + skill) and the Nix dev-shell flake moved to their own branches (`screenshot-verify`, `nix-dev-shell`) —
different review bar, split out per PR review 2026-07-06._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation FOLLOW-UP audit (FA wave) — FA-4: start-latch decision + progress-label nit + CLOSE-OUT (F-7, F-8) — on `master`. THE FA WAVE IS COMPLETE (4 of 4).** The working paper is folded into `docs/architecture.md` "Translation sidecar — design record" (a "Follow-up audit (FA wave) — outcomes" paragraph + per-finding disposition F-1…F-8 that keeps the `F-N`/`FA-N` code-comment anchors resolvable) and DELETED (`git rm docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md`; git history keeps it) per the doc-lifecycle rule._
_**F-7 (a transient start failure permanently disables translation for the session) — SHIPPED as option (c); option (a) rejected-with-reason.** The ~10 GB sidecar's lazy start arms the permanent `startFailed` latch on any non-bind-race failure (the reranker precedent); for this model the likeliest cause is transient memory pressure from the co-resident chat model, latching translation off until restart with only "runtime failed" to show. **DECISION — verified against `LlamaServer`'s real start surface first:** no reliable transient/OOM signature exists across OSes to safely un-latch — the only machine-readable signal is the exit code, and on Windows the OOM/`std::bad_alloc` exit `0xC0000409` COLLIDES with the PERMANENT #20305 minja template crash (documented in `TRANSLATION_TEMPLATE_ARGS`), while a Linux OOM-kill `SIGKILL` is indistinguishable from our own `stop()`. So **option (a) "classify OOM-shaped failures as non-latching like bind races" is REJECTED** (it would also un-latch a genuinely permanent fault sharing the signature → every window re-spawns + re-awaits the full health timeout, the exact cost the latch exists to prevent); option (b) time-bounding shares that reliance — not chosen. **Shipped (c):** keep the latch (correct for the permanent case) but tag its error with a distinct code — a new `TranslationStartError`/`TRANSLATION_START_FAILED_CODE` in `runtime.ts` (the bind-race exemption UNCHANGED: a bind race still propagates raw, non-latching). The two consumers map it to actionable "restart the app / free memory" copy: the view path (`jobs.ts`) surfaces a new `TranslateErrorCode` `startFailed` → `translate.err.startFailed`; the doc-task path (`handlers/translation.ts`) rethrows it as `main.translation.startFailed` (added to the manager's `FRIENDLY_TASK_ERROR_KEYS` so it passes through to `status.error`, shown verbatim by the file view). Both new keys are en+de, parity-compile-enforced. Content-free: only the code/localized copy cross to the renderer (no cause message); the added local `log.warn`s log the runtime/stderr string, the same content-free class `security-model.md` §"Translate view content-free logs" already permits — so **`security-model.md` is unchanged (confirmed)**. Both consumers also SKIP the futile retry (the latch re-throws identically). **F-8 (file progress label counted the materialize step — `stepsTotal = windows + 1`, so a 12-window doc read "(3/13)") — FIXED, display-only.** A `windowProgress()` helper in `fileTranslateSession.ts` subtracts the materialize step and clamps `windowsDone`, applied on BOTH the fresh-start poll and the FA-3 adopt seed; the doc-task's real progress contract is untouched. (The DocumentsScreen inline-translate label is a separate surface, out of scope, left as-is.) **Tests (+4):** `translation-runtime.test.ts` — a non-bind start fault latches AND carries the distinct code (one spawn), a bind race does NOT latch and is not a `TranslationStartError` (retries); `translate-ipc.test.ts` — a start failure surfaces the `startFailed` code, no retry; `doctasks-translation.test.ts` — a start failure fails the task with `main.translation.startFailed`, no per-window retry; `fileTranslateSession.test.ts` — a fresh 2-window doc shows "(1/2)" not "(1/3)"; the existing FA-3 adopt pin retargeted to the F-8-corrected total. **Suite 3584/47** (was 3580; +4). typecheck + build green. **Docs:** architecture.md FA-wave record (above) + TG-5 label note; `known-limitations.md` gains the F-7 start-failure bullet; working paper deleted. **The FA wave (F-1…F-8) is fully closed — every finding fixed or decided-with-record.** Unpushed on `master`._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation FOLLOW-UP audit (FA wave) — FA-3: file-path reload adoption + targeted cancel (F-3, F-6) — on `master`.** (Plan: [`docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md`](docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md) — FA-3 of 4; the plan stays OPEN, retired at FA-4.)_
_Scope: the renderer/doc-task seam — the file/document translation path's reload recovery + a foreign-task-safe cancel. **F-6 done FIRST (it is a dependency of a clean F-3 adoption — adoption must cancel only its OWN task).** The untargeted `cancelDocTask()` (no id → active fallback) could cancel a FOREIGN task in two narrow races (a Stop landing after our task went terminal with another screen's task now on the lane; a supersede after Stop + an immediate new start). Fix: the `cancelDocTask(jobId?)` IPC now routes a PRESENT id to a new manager `cancelActiveDocTask(id)` that cancels ONLY when the id IS the active task (running, else next-queued — the same "active" the no-arg fallback uses); a stale/foreign id is a NO-OP. An ABSENT id keeps the active-task fallback (the chat busy banner) — existing callers unchanged. The internal `cancelDocTask(id)` (cancels an exact running-OR-queued task — `cancelAllDocTasks` + the skill-tool-run rely on it) is untouched. `fileTranslateSession` now holds the started doc-task's jobId (`docTaskJobId`, cleared in the reset/clear paths) and threads it through BOTH cancel paths — the supersede-cancel (`started.jobId`) and `cancelFileTranslation` (the held id). **F-3 (the document path had no renderer-reload recovery; the text path did).** A full renderer reload kills `fileTranslateSession`'s module store + poll timers while the doc-task keeps running in main, so the Translate screen came back IDLE (no progress/Stop/result) and a fresh attempt was refused `docTaskBusy` until the invisible task finished. Fix: a new `adoptActiveFileTranslation()` mirrors the text path's `adoptActiveJob`, called from the Translate screen's mount effect ALONGSIDE it. It reads main's active doc-task over a NEW `getActiveDocTask` IPC (manager `getActiveDocTask()` → the RUNNING task's status copy, or null) and, when it is a running `translation` task, seeds `state:'translating'` + window progress with a null-tolerant `fileName` (main tracks ids only after a reload) and resumes the poll under a FRESH generation (the poll loop was extracted into a shared `pollDocTask` so start + adopt share it). Precedence with the text adopt: the file adopt no-ops when a live text job owns the panel or the active task is not a running translation; with the D9 one-at-a-time lane the two can never both claim the panel. **Tests:** `fileTranslateSession.test.ts` (+4 — running translation adopted → polls to done → result loads [null fileName seed asserted]; no active task → no-op; non-`translation` active task → no-op; live-text-job precedence → no-op); `doctasks-translation.test.ts` (+3 — the F-6 race pin: a stale jobId `cancelActiveDocTask` does NOT kill the newer active task [it runs to its own `done`]; a matching-id targeted cancel + the no-arg fallback still cancel the active task; `getActiveDocTask` returns the running translation copy / null when idle). **Suite 3580/47** (was 3573; +7). typecheck + build green. **Docs:** F-3/F-6 marked ✅ FIXED in the plan; `known-limitations.md` translation section gains a reload-recovery bullet (both paths re-adopt on reload; the file/document asymmetry closed; the fileName-unavailable-after-reload cosmetic edge); `security-model.md` unchanged (the new `getActiveDocTask` IPC is an ids-only status read, the targeted cancel is content-free — no new log content). **Next action: FA-4 (F-7 start-latch decision, F-8 nit, close-out + plan deletion).**_

_2026-07-06 — **Translation FOLLOW-UP audit (FA wave) — FA-2: retry policy + control-token sanitization widening (F-2, F-5) — on `master`.** (Plan: [`docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md`](docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md) — FA-2 of 4; the plan stays OPEN, retired at FA-4.)_
_Scope: the retry-policy + hardening pair sharing the completion/prompt seams. **F-2 (retrying a deterministic limit-stop is futile).** The sidecar decodes at temperature 0 (greedy) with `cache_prompt`, so a window that hit the output cap / a greedy repetition loop (a "limit stop", `out.length > 0 && !isCleanStop(final)`) reproduces the IDENTICAL truncation on retry — the second decode (up to ~30 min per window at the 1.1 tok/s CPU floor) was pure waste for the same marked/`runtimeFailed` outcome. Both retry loops now classify the failed attempt: retry on **throw** (the class M1 crash-recovery feeds) or **empty** (transient); fail immediately on a **non-empty limit-stop** (deterministic). `doctasks/handlers/translation.ts` `translateWithRetry` returns `null` on the limit-stop (mark now); `translation/jobs.ts` `run()` sets a `limitStop` flag that breaks the attempt loop → `fail('runtimeFailed')`. The abort-propagation contract is byte-identical — the limit-stop early-return happens only after the existing `signal.aborted` re-check, so no pending abort is swallowed; throw/empty still retry once. **F-5 (`sanitizeSourceText` covered only the 2 turn markers).** llama-server tokenizes the raw `/completion` prompt WITH special-token parsing, so a literal `<bos>`/`<eos>`/`<unk>`/`<pad>`/`<start_of_image>`/`<end_of_image>` in the source still tokenized to a real control token (not a turn-forgery escape — TA-4 M4 closed that — but a stray mid-prompt BOS/EOS degrades that window). `GEMMA_TURN_MARKER_RE` → `GEMMA_SPECIAL_TOKEN_RE` widened to the full Gemma-3 special-token family, rewritten to the same visually-identical `⟨…⟩` (U+27E8/U+27E9) non-token spelling; exact-marker alternation, so ordinary `<div>`/`<b>` HTML and lookalikes (`<bosch>`) stay untouched. A `TODO(smoke)` note asks the next `translategemma-smoke` to reconfirm the family against the pinned GGUF tokenizer config; shipped defensively from the model card meanwhile (TA-4 posture). **Tests:** the two truncation tests flipped from `calls === 2` to `1` (`translate-ipc.test.ts`, `doctasks-translation.test.ts`); the throw/empty tests stay at 2 (transient class still retries); the FA-1 multi-window rollback test retargeted from a limit-stop trigger to a throw so it still exercises F-1 under the new policy; per-marker sanitize unit cases + the ordinary-`<…>`-untouched invariant. **Docs:** F-2/F-5 marked FIXED in the plan; `known-limitations.md` translation retry-semantics bullet updated (transient → retried, deterministic truncation → marked/failed immediately); `security-model.md` unchanged (no new log content). **Suite 3573/47** (was 3570; +3 net from the widened sanitize + flipped-call assertions). typecheck + build green. **Next action: FA-3 (F-3 file-path reload adoption + F-6 targeted cancel).**_

_2026-07-06 — **Translation FOLLOW-UP audit (FA wave) — FA-1: view-job output integrity + cancel-detach (F-1, F-4) — on `master`.** (Plan: [`docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md`](docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md) — FA-1 of 4; the plan stays OPEN, retired at FA-4.)_
_Scope: the one silent-output-corruption path left plus the small IPC leak in the same flow. **F-1 (view-job retry duplicated already-streamed text).** `translation/jobs.ts` `run()`'s TA-5 M7 retry re-runs a window that came back empty/truncated/throwing, but a transiently-failed attempt has ALREADY streamed its deltas through `emitDelta` into `job.text` (and to the renderer); the retry appended the window a SECOND time and the duplication survived into the terminal `done` text the user copies. Fix: the loop now snapshots `job.text` into a `checkpoint` AFTER the `'\n\n'` window separator emit (`i > 0`) and BEFORE the attempt loop, and restores it via `this.patch(jobId, { text: checkpoint })` before each retry attempt (`attempt > 1`) — so a failed attempt's deltas are rolled back and the window appears in `done` exactly once. `patch()`'s cancelled-guard means the restore never resurrects a job cancelled mid-flight; the retry POLICY (2 attempts for empty/truncated/throw) is unchanged (that is FA-2). No new IPC — `trDone` carries the full text and the renderer replaces its output with it. **F-4 (`destroyed`-listener leaked per CANCELLED view job).** `registerTranslateIpc.ts` detached the sender's `once('destroyed')` listener only via the `emit.done`/`emit.error` terminals, but a cancelled job emits NEITHER — so every Stop-cancelled translation left one listener on the sender (→ `MaxListenersExceededWarning` at 11 for a long-lived window doing start/Stop cycles). Fix: a `jobId → detach` map (`detach()` also clears its own map entry); the `translateCancel` handler consults it by jobId (reaching the original sender though cancel is invoked with a fresh event), and the destroyed-cancel path calls `detach()` after `jobs.cancel`. **Tests:** translate-ipc.test.ts (+3 — F-1 retry-then-SUCCESS single-window [attempt-1 partial-then-throw → `done.text` carries the window ONCE], F-1 multi-window [attempt-1 partial-then-limit-stop → rollback keeps the `'\n\n'` joins, no residue]; **both confirmed RED before the fix** [15 pass/2 fail]; F-4 cancel-detaches-listener asserting `listenerCount('destroyed')` drops to 0 on cancel, parity with the TA-6 done-detach test); `tests/helpers/ipc.ts` `makeEvent` gains a `listenerCount` accessor. Existing empty/truncated retry-then-FAIL assertions stay green (retry policy untouched). `npm test` **3573 pass / 47 skip** + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Docs:** F-1/F-4 marked ✅ FIXED in the plan file (what shipped); no other doc changes (no behavior/contract change beyond the fix). **Next: FA-2** (retry policy F-2 + sanitization widening F-5). Prior FA-wave-audit entry ↓._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation FOLLOW-UP audit (FA wave) — audit COMPLETE, fixes NOT started.** A fresh post-TA-wave audit of the translation feature found 8 new items (none overlap the TA deferred backlog): **F-1 (High/Med)** view-job retry duplicates already-streamed text into the final `done` output (`translation/jobs.ts` — TA-5 M7 retry has no rollback of `job.text`; retry-then-SUCCESS untested), **F-2 (Med)** retrying a deterministic temperature-0 limit-stop is futile (~doubles a ~30-min CPU window, both consumers), **F-3 (Med)** the file/document path has no renderer-reload re-adoption (text path has `adoptActiveJob`; `fileTranslateSession` dies silently while the doc-task runs on), **F-4 (Low)** `destroyed`-listener leaks per CANCELLED view job (`registerTranslateIpc` detach only fires via done/error emits), **F-5 (Low)** `sanitizeSourceText` covers only the 2 turn markers (literal `<bos>`/`<eos>`/image markers still tokenize special), **F-6 (Low)** untargeted `cancelDocTask()` can kill a foreign task in narrow races, **F-7 (Low)** transient-OOM start failure arms the PERMANENT `startFailed` latch, **F-8 (nit)** file progress label counts the materialize step. Report + 4-phase fix plan (FA-1 output-integrity/detach → FA-2 retry-policy/sanitize → FA-3 adoption/targeted-cancel → FA-4 latch-decision/nit/close-out): [`docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md`](docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06-followup.md) — a WORKING PAPER, to be folded into architecture.md's "Translation sidecar" record and deleted at FA-4 per the doc-lifecycle rule. **Next action: FA-1.**_

_2026-07-06 — **Translation audit (TA wave) — TA-7: low sweep, doc drift, WAVE CLOSE-OUT (L9, L11, L12, L10-decision, D1–D5) — on `master`. THE TA WAVE IS COMPLETE.**_
_Scope: the last phase — three code lows, a documented decision, five doc-drift fixes, and the doc-lifecycle close-out. **L9 (window-budget backstop direction):** `translationUsableTokens` clamped `contextTokens` UP to 1024 — the wrong direction (a future manifest with a SMALLER real context would then budget every window against a larger context than the model has → overflow). Now it uses the REAL context; only a junk/≤0 read falls back to `TRANSLATION_FALLBACK_CONTEXT_TOKENS` (1024), and `translationBudgetWords` clamps DOWN to what the real context holds (never inflates). Unit case at ctx 512. **L11 (unbounded `tasks` map + wrong expired-kind):** the manager's `tasks` map grew for the whole session and the expired-id `getDocTask` fallback hardcoded `kind:'summary'`. Terminal tasks are now evicted past `DOC_TASK_MAX_TERMINAL_HISTORY` (16; running/queued never evicted), and a bounded `kindHistory` lets an evicted task's late poll echo its REAL kind. Manager eviction + kind-recovery unit tests. **L12 (persisted English strings):** `failedWindowNotice` + `translationAttributionLine` are written INTO the generated document but were hardcoded English; they now route through `tMain` (new en+de keys `main.translation.failedWindowNotice` / `.attributionLine`, parity compile-enforced) so the materialized document is in the app language at materialization time — the one deliberate persisted-string exception to the canonical-English-DB rule. Integration `de`-materialization assertion added. **L10 (ACCEPTED, no code):** `approxTokenCount` under-charges a pathological glued emoji/astral run, so "over-chunk, never overflow" is not absolute; NOT fixed in this wave (the shared-chunker blast radius is not worth it; the failure mode is an honest failed-window notice). Documented in `known-limitations.md` + the `doctasks/translation.ts` header. **Doc drift D1–D5:** packaging smoke row + smoke header (identifiers verbatim, numbers/dates localize); the prompt-reserve comment made past-tense (D3); `security-model.md` log enumeration extended with the content-free `error: String(err)` failure log (D4); three stale `HILBERTRAUM_TRANSLATION_SMOKE` → `HILBERTRAUM_TRANSLATEGEMMA_SMOKE` smoke comments corrected (D5). **Close-out (doc-lifecycle rule):** the wave outcomes + deferred backlog are folded into `docs/architecture.md` "Translation sidecar — design record" (a TA-7 bullet, a "Translation audit (TA wave) — outcomes" paragraph, and the deferred backlog); BOTH `docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06.md` (the audit report) AND `docs/translation-audit-fix-plan.md` (the fix plan) are DELETED (git history keeps them). Deferred (recorded, NOT scheduled): full emitter REBIND on `getActive` across windows (L3 shipped cancel-on-destroy only), Translate-view paste-size cap, `GeneratedProvenance` language pair, doc-task dedup on double-click, `extractSegmentTexts` abort signal, mid-segment split paragraph break, `resolveSidecarSelection` dead `makeReal`, drop-zone flicker/tab stops, file-pane a11y live region. **Ritual:** `npm test` (3505 pass / 47 skip), `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build` all green._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation audit (TA wave) — TA-6: sidecar crash recovery, single-flight teardown, sender-destroy cancel (M1, M5, L2, L3) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translation-audit-fix-plan.md` — TA-6 of 7; the plan stays OPEN, retired at TA-7.)_
_Scope: robustness of the TranslateGemma sidecar runtime + the view job's sender binding. **M1 (crash recovery) — a sidecar crash left a dead `this.server` handle until 120 s of idle quiet.** `TranslationRuntime` never observed the child dying on its own, so after a mid-session crash every `translate()` failed with a connection error and each attempt RE-ARMED the idle clock, so the outage persisted as long as attempts arrived < 120 s apart. Fix: `ensureStarted` wires `LlamaServer`'s existing **`onUnexpectedExit`** hook (it fires only for a HEALTHY child dying outside `stop()`) to null `this.server` — **identity-compared** (`if (this.server === server)`) so a late crash notice can't clobber a NEWER instance a soft teardown+restart already installed. Next `translate()` cold-starts (the doc-task's one retry gets a fresh spawn). Option (a) — the sidecar offers the callback — so the connection-error catch-path (option b) was unnecessary. **M5 (`teardown()` not single-flighted).** A quit `stop()` arriving while a suspend's kill was in its SIGTERM→2 s→SIGKILL window saw `this.server` already nulled and resolved IMMEDIATELY (app could exit with the escalation pending → orphan on POSIX); two overlapping suspends let the second's `finally` clear `tearingDown` while the first was still killing (a racing `translate()` cold-started during the vault re-encrypt). Fix: a shared **`teardownPromise`** (mirrors `idleTeardownPromise`) — an overlapping teardown awaits it instead of starting a second pass; `tearingDown` + `teardownPromise` clear together via a `.finally` on the shared promise (runs on a microtask, so the `this.teardownPromise = run` assignment always wins even when the body completes without awaiting), only once it settles. While here: a cold start now **awaits a live `idleTeardownPromise` first**, so a translate racing the soft idle teardown no longer briefly double-loads ~10 GB (waits for the dying child, then spawns ONE). **L2 (`TranslateJobService.tearingDown` was dead code).** `stop()` has no `await` (TA-5 left it synchronous), so the latch set+cleared in one synchronous block — no continuation could observe it. Removed the latch + its defense-claiming comment; the real defense (`stop()` aborts every controller's signal before the vault re-encrypts → a `run()` scheduled just before sees `signal.aborted`) is now what the guard checks and the comment states. **L3 (stream events bound to the starting `webContents` forever).** In the multi-window app a destroyed window dropped `trToken/trDone/trError` silently while the job decoded to the 45-min timeout holding the busy lane. Fix (minimal): `translateStart` listens for the sender's **`'destroyed'`** event and cancels the job (parity with lock), detached on terminal state so a long-lived window running many translations doesn't pile up one listener per call. A full emitter-**rebind** onto another window via `getActive` is deliberately **DEFERRED** (recorded in the plan's deferred backlog). **Tests:** translation-runtime.test.ts (+5 — crash-recovery cold-start, identity-guard [a late crash of child0 doesn't clobber child1], concurrent `stop()`+`suspend()` both await the shared teardown, overlapping-suspend `tearingDown` HOLD [racing translate refused with `suspending` until settle], translate-racing-soft-teardown AWAITS it [no double spawn]; the old "cold-start a fresh child" test was rewritten to the await shape); translate-ipc.test.ts (+2 — destroyed-sender cancels the active job + aborts the fetch; a completed job detaches its listener); `tests/helpers/ipc.ts` `makeEvent` is now EventEmitter-backed (adds `once`/`removeListener`/`destroy`) so handlers can wire the `'destroyed'` lifecycle. `npm test` **3501 pass / 47 skip** + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Docs:** architecture.md "Translation sidecar" record TA-6 bullet (crash path + single-flight teardown + L2/L3 outcomes; L3 recorded as cancel-on-destroy with the full rebind deferred). **Next: TA-7** (low sweep, doc drift, TA-wave close-out — L9/L11/L12/L10-decision/D1–D5; folds + deletes the plan & audit report). Prior TA-5 entry ↓._
_Scope: the final frame's stop reason is now **load-bearing** for BOTH translation consumers, closing a silent output-limit-truncation hole and a silently-dropped-window hole. **M6 (limit-stop detection) — output-limit truncation was undetectable.** The `/completion` final frame's stop reason was surfaced only as `stoppingWord` via `onFinal`, and NEITHER real consumer passed `onFinal` — so any non-empty reply counted as a clean window. A greedy-decode repetition loop (the classic temperature-0 MT pathology) or a token-dense window running to the ~2,070-token cap was stitched mid-sentence into the persisted document **silently**. Fix: `CompletionFinal` gained **`stoppedEos`** + a shared **`isCleanStop(final)`** helper next to it in [services/translation/completion.ts] (clean iff a non-empty `stopping_word` OR the eos flag; a MISSING final frame is already `IncompleteStreamError` from TA-4, so callers only reach `isCleanStop` with a real frame). Both consumers now thread `onFinal` and treat a non-clean (LIMIT) window as a FAILED attempt: the doc-task `translateWithRetry` ([handlers/translation.ts]) folds it into the EXISTING retry-then-`failedWindowNotice` path (empty OR truncated → marked window). `onFinal` threading was chosen over resolving `{text,final}` — the smaller diff, and it keeps the manual smoke's runtime-layer `onFinal` consumer working. **M7 (view job silently dropped an empty/truncated window).** The doc-task's retry-then-mark policy lived only in the doc-task handler; the view loop ([services/translation/jobs.ts]) accepted whatever each `translate()` resolved, so one transiently empty (or, post-M6, truncated) window in a multi-window paste completed **"done" with a silently missing/cut paragraph**. Fix: the view window loop now retries an empty/non-clean-stop/throwing window ONCE and, if it still fails, fails the WHOLE job with the existing **`runtimeFailed`** shape (the interactive view surfaces a visible failure — no new partial-result UI per the plan). A user cancel still aborts `signal` and lands as `cancelled`, never a failed window; the genuinely-empty-INPUT fast path is unchanged (rejected `badRequest` at start). **Test-gap #4 (timeout ≠ cancel, PINNED).** A per-request timeout aborts the COMBINED signal (`combineSignals`) with a `TimeoutError` while the task's OWN signal stays unaborted, so `isAbortError(err, taskSignal)` is false and the window is retried-then-failed — never mistaken for a user cancel. This hung on the abort reason's *name* and nothing pinned it; now pinned. **Tests:** doctasks-translation.test.ts (+2 — a scripted-translator window-2 LIMIT stop [`final:()=>{}`, no stopping word] → retried once → `failedWindowNotice` for window 2, other windows intact; the `TimeoutError` single-window run → 2 calls → task fails friendly, not `cancelled`); translate-ipc.test.ts (+2 — empty-window retry → `runtimeFailed`; truncated-window [`onFinal({})`] retry → `runtimeFailed`, both asserting the one retry); translation-completion.test.ts (+5 — `isCleanStop` truth table incl. eos-only + missing-final, and `stopped_eos` surfaced from the terminal frame); the scripted translators in doctasks-translation / translate-ipc / audit-ipc now report a CLEAN stop via `onFinal` by default (a normal resolve ALWAYS carries a terminal frame — modeling reality), the doctasks helper gaining a `final?` hook to script a limit stop. Manual smoke ([tests/manual/translategemma-smoke.test.ts]) header notes `stopping_word` is now load-bearing (no behavior change — a real within-budget window ends on `<end_of_turn>`). `npm test` **3495 pass / 47 skip** + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Docs:** known-limitations.md translation section (the "marked, never dropped" bullet now covers TRUNCATION — detected via the final stop reason; doc-task marks, view fails visibly; the pre-TA-5 silent-stitch honesty hole called out as closed); architecture.md "Translation sidecar" record TA-5 bullet (M6 `isCleanStop`/`stoppedEos` + `onFinal` threading, M7 view retry-then-`runtimeFailed`, test-gap #4). **Next: TA-6** (sidecar runtime robustness — M1, M5, L2, L3). Prior TA-4 entry ↓._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation audit (TA wave) — TA-4: SSE reader terminal-frame + error-field handling, control-token sanitization (M2, M3, M4, L1, L4) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translation-audit-fix-plan.md` — TA-4 of 7; the plan stays OPEN, retired at TA-7.)_
_Scope: five defects in the raw `/completion` SSE reader ([services/translation/completion.ts]) + the prompt builder ([services/translation/prompt.ts]) — a silent-truncation class + a control-token-forgery injection gap. **M2 (sawFinal) — a stream ending WITHOUT the terminal `stop:true` frame counted as success.** `readCompletionSSE` treated reader `done` (or a flushed tail) with no final frame as normal termination, so `runTranslate` resolved the accumulated PARTIAL as the full translation — a server-side close mid-decode yielded a silently-truncated "success" (the view emitted `trDone` with cut text; the doc-task handler accepted the non-empty output and **materialized a truncated document**). Fix: the reader now tracks `sawFinal` and, if the stream ends without it **and the caller did NOT abort**, throws a NEW **`IncompleteStreamError`** (a `CompletionError` subtype — deliberately NOT an `AbortError`, so both consumers hit their EXISTING failure paths: the view job's `catch`→`runtimeFailed`, the doc-task `translateWithRetry`→retry-then-`failedWindowNotice`; `isAbortError` returns false for it). **M3 (error-field) — `error:`-field SSE lines were dropped.** `parseCompletionLine` only handled `data:` lines; llama.cpp can emit a mid-stream failure as a bare `error: {…}` SSE FIELD line (compounding M2 — a hard server error became silent truncation). Fix: it now recognizes `error:`-prefixed lines (nested `{error:{…}}` OR a bare `{message,type}` payload) and maps them to `CompletionError`; even an unparseable error field is surfaced (generic `CompletionError`), never swallowed. A manual-smoke TODO ([tests/manual/translategemma-smoke.test.ts] header) records confirming the pin's ACTUAL error framing at the next smoke run (needs drive access — NOT gating this phase; the handling is defensive-both-shapes). **M4 (control-token sanitization) — Gemma turn-marker forgery via source text.** `buildTranslationPrompt` interpolated the source text RAW, and llama-server tokenizes `/completion` prompts WITH special-token parsing — a document containing a literal `<start_of_turn>`/`<end_of_turn>` forged a turn boundary (the D2 "embedded instructions are translated, never obeyed" guarantee only covered PLAIN-TEXT imperatives). Fix: new **`sanitizeSourceText`** rewrites the two Gemma turn markers to a visually-identical NON-token spelling using mathematical angle brackets — `⟨start_of_turn⟩`/`⟨end_of_turn⟩` (U+27E8/U+27E9): reversible-safe, human-legible (the translated form of a control marker is best-effort regardless), and confined to those exact two markers so ordinary `<…>` content (HTML/code) is untouched. The builder's own scaffold markers are appended AFTER the rewrite, so they survive. **L1 (abort throws) — an abort between token deliveries resolved partial text as success.** The generator's `if (signal?.aborted) return` ended cleanly, letting `translate()` resolve a partial (violating the "resolves with the full text" contract; latent because both consumers re-check the signal). Fix: on abort the reader now THROWS the caller's abort reason (or a matching `AbortError`) instead of returning — matching what an in-`read()` abort already throws. **L4 (garbled-frame counter) — misleading catch comment + silent drop.** The parse-failure branch was commented "partial frames", but the `\n`-splitter only feeds COMPLETE lines, so the only thing it swallows is a genuinely garbled frame. Fix: the comment is corrected and the reader now counts such frames and logs a single content-free `log.warn('translation SSE: dropped unparseable frame', { count })` per stream (the COUNT only, never the frame text — the privacy rule). **Tests (closes audit test-gap #3):** NEW `tests/unit/translation-completion.test.ts` (16 — drives `readCompletionSSE` directly with scripted `ReadableStream`s: multi-chunk mid-line splits, CRLF, no-trailing-newline flush, terminal→`onFinal`, `data:`-error throws, `error:`-field throws + nested + unparseable [M3], stream-end-without-final throws [M2], IncompleteStreamError-is-not-AbortError, abort-already/mid-stream throws [L1], garbled-frame skipped+counted+accumulated + clean-stream-no-warn [L4]); `translation-prompt.test.ts` (+2 — embedded turn markers neutralized [only the 2 scaffold `<start_of_turn>` + 1 `<end_of_turn>` remain raw]; `sanitizeSourceText` rewrites only the two markers, leaves ordinary `<div>`/`<b>` intact); `translation-runtime.test.ts` (+1 — no-terminal-frame stream → retryable reject at the runtime layer; the existing `COMPLETION_SSE` fixtures already carry the terminal frame, so M2 leaves them green). `npm test` **3486 pass / 47 skip** + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Docs:** security-model.md "Translate view" (new bullet — D2 prompt-injection containment now covers control tokens, not just plain-text imperatives, via `sanitizeSourceText`); architecture.md translation record TA-4 bullet (sawFinal/M2 + error-field/M3 + sanitization/M4 + L1/L4). **Next: TA-5** (stop-reason surfacing + window-failure policy — M6, M7; depends on this phase's `onFinal`/`CompletionFinal`). Prior TA-3 entry ↓._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation audit (TA wave) — TA-3: renderer store hardening — per-timer poll latch, post-picker guard, stop/error edges (H4, M8, L5–L8) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translation-audit-fix-plan.md` — TA-3 of 7; the plan stays OPEN, retired at TA-7.)_
_Scope: six renderer-store defects in the two Translate session stores + the drop zone; all behavior-preserving hardening (no doc changes beyond this entry). **H4 (fileTranslateSession) — the `pollInFlight` reentrancy latch was module-level and force-released.** `setInterval` fires every POLL_MS regardless of round-trip latency, so a latch guards against overlapping poll callbacks; but the shared latch was reset by `stopPolling()` AND each callback's `finally { pollInFlight = false }` ran even on the stale-generation early-returns. Slow `getImportJob`/`getDocTask` round-trip → Stop → new file dropped → the OLD generation's callback resolving freed the NEW generation's in-flight latch → two ticks fired concurrently → both saw `done` → **double `startTranslationTask` → the 2nd `startDocTask` refused (task active) → `failWith` killed the surviving timer while the real backend task ran unwatched (a zombie holding the one-at-a-time lane, materializing a document)**. Fix: the latch is now **LOCAL to each `setInterval` closure** (`let inFlight` captured per timer) and the reset is deleted from `stopPolling()` — a stale callback only ever frees its own (already-dead) timer's latch. **M8 (fileTranslateSession) — `guardStart()` was run only BEFORE the picker await.** A text translation starting while the OS dialog was open (non-modal: no focused BrowserWindow, or a Linux WM) let `runImport` call `clearTranslateSession()` — dropping the text store WITHOUT cancelling the main-side job (an orphan on the lane; a later remount re-adopted a both-sessions-active state). Fix: `translatePickedFile` **re-runs `guardStart()` after the await resolves with a path**, before `runImport`; on refusal it returns the outcome (`'busy'`) and leaves the text session untouched (mirroring how a rejected drop keeps the text result). **L5 (translateSession) — Stop swallowed during the start round-trip.** `stopActive()` early-returned while `activeJobId` was null, but Stop shows as soon as `translating` is true (before `translateStart` resolves). Fix: when `translating` with no `activeJobId`, `stopActive()` bumps `startGen` + sets `cancelled`, so the in-flight start's post-await supersede branch cancels its own just-started job (the existing supersede-cancel). **L6 (translateSession) — busy-guard asymmetry + unguarded `job.error` deref.** (a) `translate()`'s entry guard checked only `activeJobId` — now a cross-session guard (parity with `guardStart`): refuse on `activeJobId || translating`, on `getFileTranslate().busy`, or on a non-terminal foreign doc task. (b) `job.error` was dereferenced OUTSIDE any try, so a malformed/undefined bridge resolve threw out of `translate()` (whose `.then` caller has no `.catch`) and wedged the store stuck `translating` with a dead Stop; the resolve handling is now wrapped + `job`-guarded so any bad shape becomes a `runtimeFailed` that resets `translating`. (translateSession now imports `getActiveDocTask`/`isDocTaskTerminal` from `./doctasks` + `getFileTranslate` from `./fileTranslateSession` — a call-time-only circular import with fileTranslateSession, harmless.) **L7 (TranslateScreen) — `onCopy` unhandled rejection:** added a `.catch` to the `copyToClipboard(...)?.then?.` chain (swallow, matching `onExport`). **L8 (TranslateDropZone) — busy zone still advertised itself as a drop target.** While busy/disabled `onDragOver` still `preventDefault()`-ed (OS copy cursor) but `onDrop` silently discarded. Fix: a shared `isFileDrag` gate on `e.dataTransfer.types.includes('Files')` — for a busy or non-file drag, set `dropEffect='none'` and do NOT `preventDefault()` (OS shows no-drop), don't highlight, and `onDrop` discards belt-and-braces. **Tests (closes audit test-gap #2):** fileTranslateSession (+9 — the H4 slow-round-trip double-fire regression on fake timers + deferred `getImportJob` [asserts exactly ONE `startDocTask`; **verified it FAILS 2≠1 against a reintroduced shared latch**], the M8 pick-await interleave [text job starts mid-dialog → `'busy'`, text session untouched], doc-task terminal `failed`-with-message/`failed`-fallback/`cancelled`, import-poll + doc-task-poll rejection → `failWith`, `previewDocument` throwing, `completed===0` unsupported); translateSession (+3 — L5 Stop-during-start-round-trip cancels the job, L6a busy-guard during the start round-trip, L6b malformed-resolve-doesn't-wedge); new `TranslateDropZone.test.tsx` (6 — file-drag highlights, non-file drag no-drop, busy no-drop + discarded drop, non-file drop discarded, file drop delivers, Enter/Space keyboard activation idle vs inert-while-busy). The existing `dropOnZone` screen-test helper now carries `types:['Files']` for the L8 gate. `npm test` **3467 pass / 47 skip** + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Docs:** none beyond this entry (behavior-preserving). **Next: TA-4** (SSE reader + prompt hardening — M2, M3, M4, L1, L4). Prior TA-2 entry ↓._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation audit (TA wave) — TA-2: renderer session-store purge moved to the real lock seam (H3) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translation-audit-fix-plan.md` — TA-2 of 7; the plan stays OPEN, retired at TA-7.)_
_Scope: the renderer lock purge of the translate + vision session stores was **dead code**. `clearTranslateSession()`/`clearFileTranslate()`/`clearVisionSession()` were called only from per-screen `useEffect`s gated on a component-state `locked` flag (read from `getAppStatus().workspaceReady`). But screens render one-at-a-time and `App.lockNow` swaps the whole shell to `WorkspaceGate` (unmounting every screen) the instant `lockWorkspace()` resolves — so the effect could NEVER observe `locked === true`. The three stores are **module-level by design** (a running job keeps streaming across navigation), so the source text, streamed translation, materialized document preview, and image/answer stayed resident in renderer memory for the whole locked period — contradicting each store's "dropped on lock" contract + security-model.md, and leaving a mid-stream text session stuck `translating` after unlock (main's `jobs.stop()` emits no `trError`; `adoptActiveJob` early-returns on the still-set `activeJobId`). **Fix:** a single **`purgeSessionStores()`** helper ([renderer/lib/lockPurge.ts]) calling all three `clear*` functions, invoked from `App.lockNow` right after `lockWorkspace()` resolves (next to `setWorkspace`) — the one seam where the lock actually happens. **A renderer grep confirmed `App.lockNow` is the ONLY lock initiator** — no auto-lock timer, no main-pushed lock event, no workspace-state polling (`getWorkspaceState` is read once on App mount) — so the single call covers every path into the locked gate; the helper is the extension point if another initiator is ever added. The dead `locked` purge effects **and** the confirmed-dead `locked` EmptyState branches were removed from [renderer/screens/TranslateScreen.tsx] + [renderer/screens/ImagesScreen.tsx] (their gate `workspaceReady === isUnlocked()` is the SAME signal the shell gates the whole screen on — so `locked` can never be observed while mounted; `locked` state, its `getAppStatus` read, and the now-unused `EmptyState` import in ImagesScreen dropped, the history-load effect made unconditional). Header comments in translateSession.ts / fileTranslateSession.ts / visionSession.ts that named "the screen" as the lock caller now name `App.lockNow` → `purgeSessionStores`. The i18n keys `translate.locked`/`images.locked` are left in place (harmless, unrendered). **Tests:** new `tests/renderer/lockPurge.test.ts` (2 — purge empties all three stores from non-idle; the stuck-busy text shape is cleared so a later `adoptActiveJob` with no active main job stays idle) + `tests/renderer/AppLock.test.tsx` (1 — drives the real flow: seed the three stores → render App in encrypted mode → click "Lock now" → `lockWorkspace` ran + all stores back to EMPTY). `npm test` **3449 pass / 47 skip** + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Docs:** security-model.md "Translate view" (the false "the renderer store calls `clearTranslateSession()` on lock" claim corrected to the App-level `purgeSessionStores` seam, with the TA-2 dead-code note); architecture.md translation record TA-2 bullet. **Next: TA-3** (renderer store hardening — H4 per-timer poll latch, M8 post-picker guard, L5–L8). Prior TA-1 entry ↓._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation audit (TA wave) — TA-1: main-process lifecycle seams — quit + lock flush the doc-task pipeline (H1, H2) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translation-audit-fix-plan.md` — TA-1 of 7; the plan stays OPEN, retired at TA-7.)_
_Scope: the two lifecycle seams where a running/queued translation doc-task could write plaintext during teardown. **H1 (quit):** `performShutdown` ([shutdown.ts]) aborted the deep-index build + the Translate-view job but NEVER the doc-task — asymmetric with the lock path. On quit mid-translation `translator.stop()` killed the in-flight window, retries failed fast against the `stopped` latch, and a task with an already-succeeded window proceeded to `materializeDocument` DURING teardown → a half-translated plaintext `.parse` transient racing the DB close (plaintext on the drive until the next-launch sweep on a hard exit between the write and the `finally` shred). Fix: `performShutdown` now calls `ctx.docTasks.cancelAllDocTasks()` in the same best-effort block that aborts the build (BEFORE the sidecars stop) and awaits the running task's `awaitActiveTaskSettled()` (bounded ~5 s `Promise.race`) next to `awaitInFlightStreamsSettled`, so the abort-unwind's materialize/shred finishes while `ctx.db` is open. **H2 (lock):** `registerWorkspaceIpc` called `cancelDocTask()` (ACTIVE task only); the DB stays open while the lock handler awaits the sidecar suspends, so when the cancelled task settled `manager.pump()` dequeued the NEXT queued translation INTO the lock window — decrypting document text to a `.parse` transient and cold-starting a fresh ~10 GB sidecar that outlived the lock (the old comment's "still-queued tasks fail friendly at dequeue — `getDb()` throws while locked" was FALSE during the handler itself). Fix: it now calls `cancelAllDocTasks()` (running + queued) and awaits `awaitActiveTaskSettled()` (bounded) before purge/lock, mirroring the stream-settle treatment. **New `DocTaskManager` seams:** `cancelAllDocTasks()` walks the running id + a queue snapshot and reuses per-id `cancelDocTask` (each queued task → terminal `cancelled`, dequeued synchronously so it NEVER reaches `running` — its plaintext-decrypting handler never runs), holds NO permanent latch (`pump` is driven per `startDocTask` → manager usable again after unlock); `awaitActiveTaskSettled()` exposes the tracked `run(task)` dispatch promise (`runningPromise`, resolves immediately when idle), bounded by the callers. **Tests:** shutdown ([tests/unit/shutdown.test.ts]) — `cancelAllDocTasks` before the sidecar stops + the settle awaited between `runtime.stop` and `lock` (ordering), plus a deferred-settle test proving lock() blocks on the unwind; workspace-ipc ([tests/integration/workspace-ipc.test.ts]) — the lock handler calls `cancelAllDocTasks` (not `cancelDocTask`) + awaits `awaitActiveTaskSettled`, plus a deferred-settle ordering test (DB stays open until the settle resolves); manager ([tests/integration/doctasks-translation.test.ts]) — `cancelAllDocTasks` flushes a running A + queued B (B goes queued→cancelled without ever running, manager reusable after), and `awaitActiveTaskSettled` resolves on settle / immediately when idle. `npm test` **3446 pass / 47 skip** + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Docs:** security-model.md (lock-path doc-task flush + settle-await; the false "fail friendly at dequeue" claim corrected; TG-4 `cancelDocTask`→`cancelAllDocTasks` mention); architecture.md translation record TA-1 bullet (H1/H2 + the new seams). **Next: TA-2** (renderer lock purge is dead code — H3). Prior TA-wave-OPEN entry ↓._

_2026-07-06 — **Translation audit (TA wave) — OPEN: audit done, fix plan created, no fix phase implemented yet.** A four-lens adversarial audit of the shipped TranslateGemma wave found 4 High / 8 Medium / 12 Low defects + 5 doc-drift items (all High and load-bearing Medium findings hand-verified against the code): report `docs/translation-audit-2026-07-06.md`, phased fix plan `docs/translation-audit-fix-plan.md` (**TA-1…TA-7**, self-contained per phase — read the plan's "How to run a phase" section first; each phase lists exactly the files to read so a fresh session needn't re-audit). Headlines: quit doesn't cancel a running translation doc-task (H1), lock leaves the doc-task QUEUE live (H2), the renderer lock purge of the translate/vision session stores is dead code (H3, systemic), the fileTranslateSession poll latch leaks across generations (H4); a silent-truncation class in the `/completion` SSE path (M2/M3/M6); Gemma control-token forgery via source text (M4). **Owner decision 2026-07-06: TA fixes commit DIRECTLY ON `master`** (no `fix/<wave>-<phase>` branch chain this wave). Phases run in numeric order (TA-5 depends on TA-4); per-phase ritual applies; the plan + audit report are retired at TA-7 per the doc-lifecycle rule. **Next action: TA-1.** Prior TG-6 entry ↓._

_2026-07-05 — **TranslateGemma wave — TG-6: calibration, per-language evidence, closure (the LAST phase) — on `master`.** The wave is DONE; the plan `docs/translategemma-translation-plan.md` was FOLDED into design records and DELETED (git history keeps it), per the CLAUDE.md doc-lifecycle rule._
_Scope: re-measure the REAL model, bake the numbers into the planner constants + the manifest, record the promotion bar, fold the plan into design records + delete it. **Measured on the real b9849 Vulkan pin (scratch root junctioned to `D:\`, 2026-07-05)** via the extended manual smoke + `llama-tokenize`. **THE LOAD-BEARING FINDING — the shipped planner constants were UNSAFE on the Gemma tokenizer.** The chat-era `1.3` input / `2.0` output tokens-per-word (measured on Qwen3-4B, carried as "conservative defaults" through TG-3) are ~HALF the real Gemma weight: `llama-tokenize` over realistic office prose gives INPUT en 1.11 · de 1.43 · nl 1.65 · uk 2.13 · pl 2.19 · **cs 2.26** tok/word (a token-dense 20-word invoice line peaks ~2.8), and measured OUTPUT (word-sparse German source → token-dense targets) en→de 1.39 · de→pl 1.79 · de→uk 1.90 · **de→cs 1.96** tok/source-word (dense short samples ~3.06). At the old constants a full ~1,150-word window would have been ~3,200+ input tokens ALONE — overflowing BOTH the 2K trained input AND the launched 4096 context (silent truncation, a latent bug since TG-3). **Fix ([doctasks/translation.ts]):** a NEW translation-specific **`TRANSLATION_INPUT_TOKENS_PER_WORD = 2.5`** (deliberately NOT the shared `SUMMARY_TOKENS_PER_WORD = 1.3`, which stays the CHAT model's summary factor for a DIFFERENT tokenizer) + **`TRANSLATION_OUTPUT_TOKENS_PER_WORD = 3.0`** (was 2.0) — both conservative CEILINGS over the measured maxima, so a window can only ever OVER-chunk (harmless), never overflow. `translationBudgetWords`/`planTranslationWindows` now split by the new weights; the D4 `TRANSLATION_MAX_INPUT_TOKENS = 1800` clamp (unchanged) binds in REAL tokens at 2.5 tok/word. Consequence: windows shrink to **~690 words** at ctx 4096 (`windowMaxTokens` ≈2,071) — the honest cost of the heavy tokenizer; the doc-task suite's "fit property" proves input+output ≤ usable ctx at every size. **RSS:** sidecar-alone ≈9.3 GiB; **co-residency ≈13.2 GiB** (translation ≈9.22 + E5 embedder ≈0.14 + a 4B chat ≈3.89, all warm — the materialize-step shape) → manifest **`recommended_min_ram_gb` 14→17** (the model-benchmarks §4 peak+3-headroom rule applied to the 13.24 GiB co-residency floor, which excludes the Electron shell — a 16 GiB box is correctly gated out of the translation + small-chat case; a 12B chat resident wants the recommended 32; two 12B models can't co-reside on 16 GB — an honest finding on the 15.8 GB test box). **Decisions revisited (recorded either way):** **D8 (GPU)** — KEEP CPU-pinned for v1 (~3–4 tok/s nominal, 1.1–4.4 across the run; tolerable for a background doc-task with progress + cancel; the only smoke drive is Windows Vulkan where #25142 is the live risk; GPU deferred behind a GPU-drive re-smoke, `TRANSLATION_DEVICE_ARGS` stays the one-line flip). Per-window timeout recalibrated **30→45 min** (`DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 2_700_000`): a ~2,070-token full window at the observed-worst ~1.1 tok/s is ~30 min, so 45 min never false-kills a live slow decode while still bounding a true hang. **D9 (chat-during-translation relaxation)** — KEEP serialization (the ≈13.2 GiB co-residency is the reason: two large models decoding at once is infeasible on target hardware). **Smoke harness extended** ([tests/manual/translategemma-smoke.test.ts]): per-language round-trip + verbatim-token check for the curated 10 (all pass — invoice no. + model code verbatim, numbers LOCALIZED, injection resisted, no `<end_of_turn>` leak — the recorded evidence the widened `TranslationLangCode` cites), `/tokenize` tokens-per-word, and a co-residency-RSS leg (translation + E5 + a resident chat); packaging.md harness-matrix row updated. **V4 re-confirmed:** `verify-models --generate` on the real file → sha256 `b7aac4b4…a528` VERIFIED. **Doc lifecycle (plan folded + DELETED):** [docs/architecture.md] "Translation sidecar — design record" is now the COMPLETE record (STUB→final) with a **§-anchor legend** + a D1–D10 decision table that keep the ~40 in-code `plan §N`/`DN`/`ON`/`VN` citations resolvable; [docs/model-benchmarks.md] **§11** (translation model measurements + the promotion bar — public MT scores are NOT a signal, consistent with §9); [docs/model-policy.md] "The translation role" finalized as the license/research record (license_review.status stays `pending` per O1 — owner's separate commercial review); [docs/known-limitations.md] refreshed (window ~690 words, ~3–4 tok/s CPU / GPU-deferred). `npm test` **3442 pass** + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Adversarially reviewed** (multi-agent, 4 dimensions × verify → 5 findings fixed: the load-bearing one was min-RAM honesty **16→17**, plus stale comments). Commit: `docs+fix(translation): TG wave calibration, per-language record, design records (TG-6)` (`4571a70`). **The TranslateGemma wave (TG-1…TG-6) is COMPLETE.**
_Follow-up (same day, on `master`): **(a) multi-window END-TO-END on the real model** — a new permanent smoke leg ([tests/manual/translategemma-smoke.test.ts] "multi-window document") plans a ~1,134-word German doc into **2 windows** at the TG-6 sizing and translates BOTH on the real b9849 sidecar: window 1 = 1153 out-tokens, window 2 = 804, **both well under the 2071 cap → no truncation** (the over-chunk-never-overflow property, proven on the real model, not just the scripted-translator integration test), with the first + last reference codes (`REF-4001`/`REF-4070`) surviving the stitch. **(b) CPU safety-net binary — pre-ship gap, fetched + digest-confirmed, RUN still pending.** The pinned pure-CPU b9849 build (`llama-b9849-bin-win-cpu-x64.zip`) was fetched and **SHA-256 == `fa7d9d93…4352`, EXACT match** to the runtime-sources pin (so [model-manifests/runtime-sources.yaml] now records the win-cpu digest as locally confirmed 2026-07-05; win-vulkan is the D:\ pin; mac/linux still pending). But EXECUTING it is blocked: Windows Defender quarantines the freshly-downloaded, unsigned `llama-server.exe` on run (only that exe was removed; every sibling exe survived), and an AV exclusion needs admin unavailable here. The substantive CPU DECODE path is already fully covered — every TG run uses `--device none` (pure CPU) on the b9849 server, and the CPU-only binary shares identical server/template/tokenizer code (differs only in omitting the Vulkan backend) — so the residual is narrow: run the `cpu-safety-net` leg on a drive/CI where the CPU build ships AV-allowlisted before release. Prior TG-5 entry ↓._

_2026-07-05 — **TranslateGemma wave — TG-5: document drag-and-drop translation in the Translate view (plan §2 D7) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translategemma-translation-plan.md` — TG-5 of 6; the LAST view-feature phase before TG-6 calibration/closure; plan stays OPEN, retired at TG-6.)
Scope: a **drop zone + "choose a document" button** under the Translate view's input pane, so a dropped/picked FILE is translated — riding the EXISTING translation **doc-task** (NOT the live TEXT job). No new parsing/IPC path; no source-language auto-detect; image translation stays out of scope (§6). **Drop zone** ([renderer/translate/TranslateDropZone.tsx], the `ImageDropZone` template — focusable, drag-over state, a WCAG 2.5.7 "choose a document" button, multi-drop rejected, disabled while busy). **Flow (D7):** `getDroppedFilePath` (the preload `webUtils.getPathForFile` bridge; a browser-origin drag with no OS path resolves to '' → friendly `noPath`) **or** `pickDocuments('files')` (carries the one-time `pickerToken` back, D1) → **`importDocuments(paths, {destination:{kind:'temporary'}})`** → poll `getImportJob` → **`startDocTask('translation', docId, {sourceLang,targetLang})`** → poll `getDocTask` → on done load the materialized doc's Markdown (bounded `previewDocument`) into the SAME output panel + actions **Export** (`exportDocument`) / **Show in Documents** (`onNavigate('documents')`) / Copy / "Translate another document" (reset). Provenance/audit/encryption ride the doc-task for free. **New renderer store [lib/fileTranslateSession.ts]** (the `translateSession` template — module-level, survives navigation) owns import + doc-task polling (own `setInterval` POLL_MS 400) + the result load, with the `gen` supersede/generation guard; `clearFileTranslate()` on lock. **ONE output panel + ONE busy state (RECORDED decision):** the screen renders the live text stream OR the file result — panel ownership = **"file if `fileTx.state !== 'idle'`, else text"** (each path resets the other only when it actually STARTS, so at most one session is non-idle → remount-safe, no component-local flag); the screen's single `busy = textTranslating || fileTx.busy` disables BOTH triggers. The file path is a REAL `ctx.docTasks` task, so the text path's backend `hasActiveDocTask()` guard blocks it too (D9 lane); the store's `guardStart` additionally refuses while a text stream runs or a NON-terminal foreign doc task holds the lane. **Progress:** window-count `Translating… (3/12)` from the doc-task `stepsDone/stepsTotal` (the TG-4 text path deliberately showed none). **Temporary-source lifecycle (documented):** the source imports as a **Temporary** doc (never Library); the translation materializes as a **Generated** doc (zero membership, findable under Documents). We do NOT bespoke-delete the temporary source — it rides the existing Temporary-lifecycle retention (owner-gated, Phase E.2); bespoke deletion would risk the source of a re-runnable translation. **Deliberate deviation from the plan's "poll (lib/doctasks.ts store)":** the new store runs its OWN import + doc-task polling rather than the GLOBAL `doctasks` store's `startTask`, so the result load never races a foreign `acknowledgeDocTask` from DocumentsScreen/ChatScreen; the backend still enforces one-task-at-a-time, so the D9 lane holds (it READS `getActiveDocTask()` only to pre-block on a foreign RUNNING task). i18n: `translate.drop.*` + `translate.file.*` (progress/actions/truncated/errors) en+de (parity compile-enforced). **Adversarial multi-agent review of the diff (Workflow, 3 lenses × verify; 4 CONFIRMED, 3 refuted) → all 4 fixed BEFORE commit:** (C1) both poll loops had no reentrancy guard — a getImportJob/getDocTask round-trip slower than POLL_MS let two overlapping `setInterval` callbacks double-fire the (non-idempotent) terminal transition → a duplicate `startDocTask` (queued FIFO → the source translated TWICE + a stray Generated doc) and a killed next-phase timer → added a `pollInFlight` latch; (C2/C3) **Stop during the `importing→translating` startDocTask round-trip** left the state `'importing'`, so `cancelFileTranslation` skipped `cancelDocTask` and the just-started backend task ran on as a **zombie** (materialized an unexpected doc, held the lane) → the post-await supersede branch now cancels the orphan (the `translateSession` supersede-cancel, applied to the doc-task lane); (C4) `guardStart` used bare truthiness on `getActiveDocTask()`, so a **terminal-but-unacknowledged** foreign task lingering in the global store spuriously refused a translation with "A document task is running" → now gated on `!isDocTaskTerminal(status)` (matching the backend `hasActiveTask()`). Plus a self-review find fixed first: a REJECTED drop/pick (multiDrop/noPath/unsupported/docTaskBusy) used to discard an existing completed TEXT result because the screen cleared it before validating → `clearTranslateSession()` moved into the store's `runImport` (only on a real start), so a rejected drop keeps the text result behind the error banner. 1 low residual ACCEPTED: `snapshot.busy` isn't set during the `pickDocuments` await, so the store-level D9 guard leans on the OS file dialog's modality for that window (unreachable via the UI — the dialog blocks the renderer; the drop path is synchronous into `runImport`). **Tests:** `fileTranslateSession.test.ts` (11 — happy drop→import(temporary)→task→materialize, picker+token, multiDrop/noPath/unsupported rejects, foreign-RUNNING docTaskBusy, C4 terminal-foreign not-blocked, C2/C3 Stop-cancels-orphan, busy-reject, cancel+reset, clear-on-lock); `TranslateScreen.test.tsx` (+6 — drop→output, picker→output, multi-drop reject, unsupported→friendly, Export + Show-in-Documents, truncated hint, rejected-drop-keeps-text); `doctasks-translation.test.ts` (+1 integration — a Temporary-imported source translates to a Generated doc, source stays Temporary, no membership on the output). `npm test` **3442 pass / 45 skip**, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build` green. **END-TO-END VERIFIED IN THE REAL APP (CDP-driven, 2026-07-05):** built app against a scratch root junctioned to D:\ (models/runtime; repo manifests; `Translation backend selected {kind:"llama"}`). The **Übersetzen** view renders the new drop zone („Oder ein Dokument zum Übersetzen ablegen" / „oder ein Dokument auswählen" / „PDF, Word, Markdown oder Text …", screenshot). The native drop/pick inputs can't be CDP-driven (a synthetic `File` resolves to '', the OS dialog can't be clicked — noted in the task), so the file→materialized-Markdown pipeline was driven via the SAME IPC the store uses on a real doc on the real sidecar: imported `klein.txt` (DE) as **Temporär** → `startDocTask('translation', de→en)` → materialized **`klein (English).md`** (Documents shows it **Erzeugt**, „Übersetzt aus klein.txt"; the source stays **Temporär**) with body „Good morning. How are you doing today? The weather is nice. I'm drinking a coffee." + the `> Machine-translated by translategemma-12b-it-q4 — may contain errors.` attribution (screenshot). Doc-task log is **content-free** (`Document task queued/completed {jobId, kind:"translation", documentId}` — no text; grep for the source/translation strings found nothing). Junctions removed with `rmdir` afterwards (D:\ intact — gguf + runtime + docs verified). **Docs:** user-guide §7a extended with the drag-drop/choose paragraph (+ transient-text-vs-saved-document + temporary-original note); known-limitations (Temporary-source accumulation, truncated in-panel preview); architecture.md "Translation sidecar" record + a TG-5 bullet (drop flow, one-panel/one-busy decision, deliberate deviation, temporary lifecycle); security-model.md "Translate view" section + a TG-5 bullet (dropped path inherits the doc-task content-free-audit + encrypted-temp posture, hardened paths, nothing new logged). **Next: TG-6** (calibration: Gemma-tokenizer tokens-per-word re-measure, per-language smoke for the curated 10, min-RAM/tok-sec, D8/D9 revisit; fold the plan into design records + delete it; CHANGELOG). Prior TG-4 entry ↓._

_2026-07-05 — **TranslateGemma wave — TG-4: the Translate view (streaming TEXT translation; plan §2 D6) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translategemma-translation-plan.md` — TG-4 of 6; plan stays OPEN, retired at TG-6.)
Scope: a new **7th primary** rail destination for live TEXT translation on the SAME `ctx.translator` TranslateGemma sidecar the doc-task uses — no second model. Drag-and-drop/file translation stays TG-5; no auto-detect. **Nav:** `ScreenId 'translate'` + `resolveNavTarget` case ([navigation.ts]); `NAV_TOP` entry between Documents and Images + render block ([App.tsx]); a new `translate` Icon glyph (the Lucide "languages" mark); `nav.translate` en+de. The rail is now **"7 primary + 1 utility"** (design-guidelines §2 updated with the rationale; `InformationArchitecture.test.tsx` + `rail-labels.test.ts` updated to the 8-item nav). **Main — a per-job streaming service:** `TranslateJobService` ([services/translation/jobs.ts], the vision image-job template) behind new IPC — **`translate:start`** → `{jobId}` (validates `isTranslationLangCode` + source ≠ target + non-empty text + a model present — mirroring the TG-3 server-side rules; busy-REJECTs a 2nd view job; refuses `docTaskBusy` while a doc task holds the lane), **`translate:cancel`**, **`translate:getActive`** (remount recovery), and **`STREAM.trToken/trDone/trError(jobId)`** (additive, the image-channel shape — `trDone` carries the COMPLETE text so a mid-stream dropped token self-heals). Single window = fast path; longer text is **split on blank lines into paragraph segments** then planned with the SHARED `planTranslationWindows` (`Translator.contextWindow()` + the D4 clamp — imported from doctasks/translation.ts, NOT duplicated) and streamed window-by-window (blank-line joins) into ONE output. Built in [index.ts] as `ctx.translateJobs`; `registerTranslateIpc` registered; drift-guard `ipc-lock-coverage.test.ts` updated. **Teardown (the TG-3 respawn fix, reused):** `TranslateJobService.stop()` aborts the in-flight job + purges the job map, wired to workspace LOCK (`registerWorkspaceIpc`, BEFORE `translator.suspend()`) AND quit (`shutdown.ts`, before `translator.stop()`) — a running view job's next window would otherwise lazily RESPAWN the just-suspended ~10 GB sidecar with the source text; `translate:start` is `requireUnlocked`-gated for the same reason. **Renderer:** `lib/translateSession.ts` module store (the `visionSession` template) so a running job survives navigation; `getActiveTranslateJob` re-adoption after a full renderer reload; the `startGen` supersede/generation guard (F8); `clearTranslateSession()` on lock. `TranslateScreen.tsx`: textarea + source/target native-name selects + swap + primary Translate + streamed output (plain-text live, `AssistantMarkdown` on done, `StreamAnnouncer` a11y; per-token render — no flush timer needed at the ~4 tok/s decode) + copy-to-clipboard + Stop + model-missing `EmptyState` → AI Model deep link. **D9 busy-gating decision (RECORDED):** the view job takes the SAME one-at-a-time lane as doc tasks — it REFUSES to start while `docTasks.hasActiveTask()` (`docTaskBusy`; the D9 RAM co-residency argument — 12B translate + a resident chat model + embedder — applies whether the task is a translation or a summary/compare). It does NOT block chat (a different sidecar; chat-during-translate relaxation stays TG-6). The doc-task manager is left unchanged (D9 "all other kinds unchanged"); the shared `--parallel 1` sidecar serializes the rare reverse race (a doc task started mid-view-translate) safely at the server. **Privacy (security-model.md):** the source text + translation are TRANSIENT — held only in the per-process job map + renderer store, dropped on lock/quit; ZERO audit, logs are ids+lang-pair only. **Review-workflow finds (multi-agent adversarial pass over the diff) → 2 REAL bugs fixed:** (1) a dismissed error banner REAPPEARED on remount because the failed state is persistent in the store but the dismiss flag was component-local → new store `acknowledgeError()` clears the terminal state; (2) a multi-window paste FLATTENED paragraph structure and inserted a spurious blank-line break mid-sentence at the token cut → the blank-line paragraph split above; plus 3 test-coverage gaps closed (lock + quit abort of the view job; the supersede guard). 2 low residuals accepted: (a) adopt's ~1 ms seed↔subscribe race (never coincides with completion at ~4 tok/s, recoverable via Stop); (b) **a systemic lock-handler window** (the review's slowest agent) — `isUnlocked()` is `_db !== null`, nulled only at the FINAL `workspace.lock()`, so between `translator.suspend()` and that `lock()` the handler yields (`awaitInFlightStreamsSettled`) while still "unlocked", and a `translate:start` dispatched in that narrow window passes `requireUnlocked` and can respawn the sidecar (source prompt in KV until the 120 s idle teardown). NOT a TG-4 regression — `VisionService.analyze` has the identical window; the robust fix is a cross-cutting workspace "locking-in-progress" latch (deferred; documented in security-model.md). **Tests:** `translate-ipc.test.ts` (10 — queued→done single window, multi-paragraph split→windows blank-line-joined, busy-REJECT, docTaskBusy, noModel, badRequest bad/same/empty, cancel+abort, getActive+remount, stop()-purge, locked refusal), `TranslateScreen.test.tsx` (7 — model-missing EmptyState + deep link, type→stream→copy, Stop→cancel, docTaskBusy banner, swap, same-lang disable + hint, dismissed-error no-reappear), `translateSession.test.ts` (adopt re-adoption + noop, busy guard, empty no-op, clear purge, supersede-cancel), GermanSmoke TranslateScreen entry, `workspace-ipc` + `shutdown` teardown assertions. `npm test`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build` green. **END-TO-END VERIFIED IN THE REAL APP (CDP-driven, 2026-07-05):** built app against a scratch root junctioned to D:\ (models/runtime; repo manifests). **PRESENT:** the **Übersetzen** rail item is live between Dokumente and Bilder → Von **Deutsch** / Nach **English** → typed „Guten Morgen. Wie geht es dir?" → real sidecar spawn (log `Translation backend selected {kind:"llama"}`; cold-load ~35 s then ~4 tok/s) → streamed **„Good morning. How are you?"**; the log is content-free (`Translate job started {jobId, source:de, target:en}` / `done {jobId}` — no text); Kopieren shown. **MISSING** (`HILBERTRAUM_MANIFESTS_DIR=D:\model-manifests`, no translation manifest): `Translation backend selected {kind:"none"}` → `translationAvailable:false` → EmptyState „Zum Übersetzen wird das Übersetzungsmodell auf diesem Laufwerk benötigt." + „Zum KI-Modell" deep-link → lands on KI-Modell (screenshot-verified). Junctions removed with `rmdir` afterwards (D:\ intact). **Docs:** design-guidelines §2 (7 primary + 1 utility + rationale); user-guide new „7a. Translate text" section + §4 nav map (seven destinations); security-model.md „Translate view (TG-4) — transient text, content-free logs"; architecture.md „Translation sidecar" record extended with a TG-4 bullet; the TG-2 sidecar record now cites TG-3+TG-4. **Next: TG-5** (drag-and-drop + file translation in the view). Prior TG-3 entry ↓._

_2026-07-05 — **TranslateGemma wave — TG-3: doc-task reroute onto the sidecar + the curated 10-language widening (the BREAKING replacement, plan O2/D3/D4/D5/D9) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translategemma-translation-plan.md` — TG-3 of 6; plan stays OPEN, retired at TG-6.)
Scope: `kind:'translation'` switches backend — it now runs on **`ctx.translator` (the TG-2 TranslateGemma sidecar) via the new `DocTaskDeps.getTranslator`**, never the chat runtime. **Manager:** enqueue AND dequeue require a non-null translator (new friendly copy **`main.translation.noModel`**, en+de, added to `FRIENDLY_TASK_ERROR_KEYS` + exported as `TASK_TRANSLATION_NO_MODEL_MESSAGE`); the "needs runtime" guard special-cases translation (all OTHER kinds unchanged; FIFO + chat↔task exclusion unchanged per D9); dispatch is direct (`runTranslation(task, translator, ctx)` — translation left `MODEL_TASK_HANDLERS`, whose Record type now also excludes it). **Handler (`handlers/translation.ts`):** windows plan against the SIDECAR's `contextWindow()` (4096 from the manifest — V6 re-confirmed: `resolveModelByRole('translation')` carries `recommendedContextTokens` into the launch exactly like vision/reranker), strictly sequential `translate()` calls (D9), a local sidecar-shaped `translateWithRetry` (one retry → unchanged `failedWindowNotice`; aborts propagate; an early `signal.aborted` guard keeps a cancelled task from lazily respawning the sidecar via `ensureStarted`); attribution + provenance stamp **`translator.modelId`**. The manager's chat-shaped `generateWithRetry` was DELETED (translation was its only consumer; `DocTaskCtx` now exposes `generate` only). **Window math (`doctasks/translation.ts`):** new **D4 clamp `TRANSLATION_MAX_INPUT_TOKENS = 1800`** (~2000-token card spec minus prompt scaffold) inside `translationBudgetWords` — at the launched 4096 the 1.3/2.0 split (~1,150 words in) already sits under it; the clamp binds larger contexts. Qwen-measured 1.3/2.0 constants KEPT as conservative defaults (comments note the TG-6 Gemma-tokenizer re-measure). The dead chat-model prompts — `translationSystemPrompt`/`translationWindowPrompt`/`TRANSLATION_TEMPERATURE` — are deleted with their tests + the old `tests/manual/translation-smoke.test.ts` harness (packaging.md matrix row updated; git history keeps them). **Data contracts (shared/types.ts):** canonical **`TRANSLATION_LANGUAGE_CODES`** (the curated 10: de,en,fr,es,it,pt,nl,pl,cs,uk) + `TranslationLangCode` + `isTranslationLangCode()` + **`TRANSLATION_NATIVE_NAMES`** now live in shared/ (ONE source of truth — `services/translation/prompt.ts` imports + re-exports them; guard comment rewritten to cite WMT24++ arXiv:2601.09012 + the TG-6 per-language smoke); `TranslationTargetLang` widened to the 10, new `TranslationSourceLang`, `StartDocTaskRequest.params` requires **`sourceLang`** for translation (validated server-side with targetLang; **source ≠ target also enforced** — a same-language pass would be a multi-GB no-op; `TASK_TRANSLATION_TARGET_MESSAGE` copy updated); legacy persisted `TranslationOrigin.targetLang` still parses. New **`AppStatus.translationAvailable`** (= `ctx.translator != null` in registerCoreIpc — the `ocrAvailable` twin). _Deliberate deviation from the plan's "query installed models via the existing models IPC": reading the composed handle means the UI hint can NEVER disagree with the doc-task guard (both read `ctx.translator`, resolved once at startup — a mid-session install needs an app restart either way, noted in known-limitations)._ **UI:** the DocumentsScreen two-button modal → **source+target selects** (native-name labels from shared, untranslated by design; session-local last-choice memory; UI-language-aware default; same-language pair disables the primary with a hint); DocRow's Translate item **disables when the model is missing** and a sibling **"Get the translation model…" item deep-links to the AI Model screen** (`onNavigate('models')` threaded App→DocumentsScreen→DocRow). **Review-workflow finds (multi-agent adversarial pass over the diff) → two REAL integration bugs fixed:** (1) the sidecar's per-window request timeout was 300 s — a near-budget window's CPU decode is ~620 s at the TG-2-measured 3.7 tok/s, so EVERY full window of a long document would have timed out twice into a failed-window notice → `DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` now **1 800 000** (still bounds a true hang; user cancel stays instant via the task signal); (2) **workspace lock now cancels the active non-yielding doc task** (`registerWorkspaceIpc`: `ctx.docTasks?.cancelDocTask()` next to `abortActiveBuild()`) — a running translation no longer dies with the chat runtime, and left uncancelled its next window would lazily RESPAWN the suspended sidecar with document plaintext while the vault re-encrypts (summary/compare now get a clean `cancelled` too). Stale preload `startDocTask` JSDoc refreshed. **Tests:** `doctasks-translation.test.ts` rewritten on a scripted `Translator` (18 — widened validation incl. source≠target + fr→uk accept, no-model refusal EVEN WITH a chat model, success with NO chat model + a booby-trapped `chatStream` proving the reroute, translator-disappears-while-queued dequeue re-check, sourceLang/targetLang/maxTokens plumbed per call, planner follows `contextWindow()` not `getContextTokens`, retry/all-fail/empty-reply, cancel, lease, busy-guard, encrypted, provenance with the translation model id); `doctasks-windows.test.ts` (D4 clamp tests + widened native-name titles; prompt-template tests removed); `DocumentTranslate.test.tsx` rewritten (selects + sourceLang payload, native labels, same-language disable, session memory on reopen, model-missing: disabled item + install deep-link → `onNavigate('models')`); `workspace-ipc.test.ts` + lock test (cancel active task + suspend-not-stop the translator); `audit-ipc.test.ts` sweep green on an echo TRANSLATOR (ids/kinds only, unchanged assertions); every `DocTaskManager` site gained `getTranslator` (null default); AppStatus fixtures gained `translationAvailable`. `npm test` **3398 pass / 45 skip**, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build` green. **END-TO-END VERIFIED IN THE REAL APP (CDP-driven, 2026-07-05):** built app against a scratch root junctioned to D:\ (models/runtime; repo manifests; fresh plaintext_dev workspace; NO chat model running at any point) — seeded German doc → Dokumente → ⋯ → Übersetzen → Von Deutsch/Nach English selects → real sidecar spawn (log: `Translation backend selected {kind:"llama"}`; llama-server RSS ~9.5 GiB) → materialized **`seed-doc (English).md`**, indexed, origin `{kind:'translation', modelId:'translategemma-12b-it-q4'}`, attribution line present, invoice/model codes verbatim, dates/amounts LOCALIZED (14.03.2026→March 14, 2026; 1.250,00 Euro→€1,250.00), embedded instruction TRANSLATED not obeyed. Model-MISSING leg (manifests dir without the translation role): `translationAvailable:false`, Übersetzen `aria-disabled`, „Übersetzungsmodell holen…" deep-links to KI-Modell — the friendly O2 path, screenshot-verified. **TG-6 watch items from the review:** the 2.0 output / 1.3 input tokens-per-word constants are DE/EN-measured — re-measure for the token-heavier uk/cs/pl (a dense Cyrillic window could exceed the 2K trained input in REAL tokens / brush the output cap); the per-window timeout recalibrates with measured tok/s. **Docs:** known-limitations translation section rewritten (model-REQUIRED + install path + restart-after-mid-session-install, 10 languages + evidence rule, 2K windows + terminology drift, localization-is-correct-MT); user-guide Translate block; architecture doc-task bullets + the TG-2 sidecar stub extended with a TG-3 record paragraph; packaging matrix. **Next: TG-4** (the Translate view — text translation). Prior TG-2 entry ↓._

_2026-07-05 — **TranslateGemma wave — TG-2: `services/translation/` sidecar + b9849 load smoke (the go/no-go gate) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translategemma-translation-plan.md` — TG-2 of 6; plan stays OPEN, retired at TG-6.)
