Scope: a working, tested translation sidecar; **NO caller changes** (the doc-task reroute is TG-3, the Translate view TG-4/5 — nothing consumes `ctx.translator` yet). **New module `services/translation/`:** `prompt.ts` (the app-side prompt builder — TranslateGemma runs WITHOUT `--jinja` per #20305, so the trained single-user-turn prompt is formatted in code with our own `code → English name` map + a native-name map exported for TG-3; 10 curated langs `de,en,fr,es,it,pt,nl,pl,cs,uk`; the exact rendered string is snapshot-pinned), `completion.ts` (the raw `/completion` SSE reader — bare-object frames + a terminal `stop:true` frame carrying `timings`, NOT `readChatSSE`'s `choices[].delta` shape; error-frame → `CompletionError`), `runtime.ts` (`TranslationRuntime` — own `LlamaServer`, args `--ctx-size 4096 --parallel 1 --device none`, NO `--jinja`; `/completion` with `temperature 0` + `stop:["<end_of_turn>"]` + abort plumbing; a HYBRID lifecycle = the **vision** RUNTIME-4 SOFT idle-teardown interlock (120 s, `HILBERTRAUM_TRANSLATION_IDLE_MS`) + the **reranker** `stop()`(permanent, quit)/`suspend()`(soft, lock→lazy-restart) split + `tearingDown`/bind-race-forgiving `startFailed` latches), `factory.ts` (`createSelectedTranslator` via `resolveSidecarSelection` — model→binary→weights→`null`, no mock), `index.ts` (`Translator` interface). **Wiring:** `compose-services.ts` resolves it via `resolveModelByRole('translation')` (availability-driven, no settings slot) → `AvailabilityServices.translator` → `AppContext.translator`; stopped on quit (`shutdown.ts`) + suspended on lock (`registerWorkspaceIpc`). **D8 (GPU) outcome:** the chat GPU ladder (`runtime/factory.ts`) yields a `chatStream` `ModelRuntime` bound to `RuntimeManager` — its seams don't fit a raw-`/completion` sidecar — so TG-2 ships `--device none` (CPU) per D8's fallback; `TRANSLATION_DEVICE_ARGS` is a one-line flip once the smoke's tok/s decides at TG-6. **V5 re-verified:** llama.cpp fix PR #20956 for #20305 is **still OPEN** (adds `--skip-chat-parsing` + extra content-part fields; a commenter reports it didn't fix the user-role error) — the no-jinja design stands; model-policy.md note updated. **V1:** the builder is reconstructed from the model cards (instruction text confirmed: "You are a professional {SRC} ({src}) to {TGT} ({tgt}) translator. Your goal is to accurately convey…") — the VERBATIM `tokenizer.chat_template` reconciliation is done by the smoke against the server's `/props` (the GGUF isn't in this env). **Tests:** `tests/unit/translation-prompt.test.ts` (6 — prompt snapshot, both-langs, data-not-scaffolding, stop-token, unknown-code throw, 10-lang maps) + `tests/integration/translation-runtime.test.ts` (19 — launch args assert NO `--jinja`/ctx 4096/`--parallel 1`/`--device none`, `/completion` body temp0+stop, streaming, `n_predict`, single-flight, failed-start latch, abort, non-200 + error-frame mapping, deterministic idle-teardown/reuse/cold-restart, stop-permanent/suspend-soft, no-orphan-on-stop-during-teardown, factory ladder). `npm test` **3390 pass / 46 skip**, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build` all green. **Manual smoke** `tests/manual/translategemma-smoke.test.ts` (env `HILBERTRAUM_TRANSLATEGEMMA_SMOKE=<drive root>`; row added to packaging.md matrix): composes `LlamaServer` with the production args on the Vulkan default + (when present) CPU safety-net binary, dumps `/props` chat_template for V1, asserts DE↔EN sanity + identifier verbatim-preservation + injection-resistance + no `<end_of_turn>` leak, prints tok/s + best-effort peak RSS. **✅ GO — RAN ON THE REAL b9849 VULKAN PIN (D:\, 2026-07-05) AND PASSED.** GGUF fetched directly (owner-authorized, accepting the Gemma Terms — this bypassed the in-app license-ack gate); **V3/V4 re-confirmed against the real file: size 7,300,794,112 + sha256 `b7aac4b4…a528` exact.**
  - **⚠ CRITICAL PRODUCTION FIX (smoke finding) — `--chat-template gemma` is now REQUIRED.** The b9849 server CRASHES AT STARTUP on TranslateGemma (Windows exit `0xC0000409`/std::bad_alloc) **even with NO `--jinja`**: it validates the model's EMBEDDED chat template during init and TranslateGemma's template (typed `{source_lang_code,target_lang_code}` content) crashes the probe (`render_message_to_json: Neither string content nor typed content is supported by the template`) — the **#20305 minja crash biting at INIT, not per-request**. Fix: launch with `--chat-template gemma` (new `TRANSLATION_TEMPLATE_ARGS`, folded into `TRANSLATION_SERVER_ARGS`) — the built-in legacy (non-jinja) template gives the startup probe something renderable; SAFE because `/completion` uses our raw prompt and never applies the chat template (`/props` then reports `chat_template: "gemma"`, confirmed). Integration test asserts `--chat-template gemma`; the smoke imports `TRANSLATION_SERVER_ARGS` so it can't drift. This is the load-bearing outcome — WITHOUT it the sidecar can't start on the pin.
  - **Measured artifact (D8/D10):** cold load 37 s; **decode 3.7–4.0 tok/s** on CPU (`--device none`, the D8 GPU-decision datapoint for TG-6); **peak RSS ≈ 9.50 GiB** (sidecar alone — the manifest's `recommended_min_ram_gb: 14` placeholder holds: 9.5 + ~3 headroom ≈ 12.5; TG-6 recalibrates with CO-RESIDENCY). **V1 reconciled:** DE↔EN + EN→DE produce clean professional translations, embedded instructions are TRANSLATED not obeyed, no stop-token leak; numbers/dates are correctly LOCALIZED (14.03.2026→"March 14, 2026", 1.250→"1,250") — proper MT, NOT a verbatim failure; stable identifiers (invoice `2026-0457`, model `RX-7b`) preserved verbatim both directions. CPU safety-net leg SKIPPED (no `runtime/llama.cpp/win/cpu/` on D:\ — a Vulkan-only smoke drive; re-run on a drive with the CPU build before ship). **Docs:** architecture.md "Translation sidecar — design record (STUB)"; packaging.md harness-matrix row; model-policy.md V5 + startup-crash note. **Next: TG-3** (doc-task reroute + language widening — the breaking replacement); the gate is GREEN. Prior TG-1 entry ↓._

_2026-07-05 — **TranslateGemma wave — TG-1: `translation` manifest role + TranslateGemma 12B manifest + surfaces (inert; no behavior change) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/translategemma-translation-plan.md` — TG-1 of 6; the plan stays OPEN, retired into design records at TG-6.)
Scope: everything needed for the model to be **discoverable, downloadable, verifiable** — nothing consumes it yet (the sidecar/doc-task/Translate-view land in TG-2…TG-5). **New role:** `ModelRole` + `ROLES` gain `'translation'` ([shared/manifest.ts]); mirrored in `ModelInfo.role` ([shared/types.ts]) and the `Record<ModelRole,number>` per-role download cap ([assets.ts] `MODEL_WEIGHT_MAX_BYTES`, 40 GiB tier like chat/vision). Install-state + discovery are role-agnostic (verified — no code change in [models.ts]); `selectModel` **refuses** it ("used automatically", like reranker/transcriber/vision); `resolveModelByRole` is already role-agnostic. **Drive layout:** `models/translation` + `model-manifests/translation` added to `DRIVE_LAYOUT_DIRS` ([drive.ts]) and BOTH `prepare-drive.ps1` `$Dirs` / `prepare-drive.sh` `DIRS` (script-drift test enforces the lockstep) + `docs/drive-layout.md`. **Manifest:** `model-manifests/translation/translategemma-12b-it-q4.yaml` (plan §2 D10) — rank 0, profiles `[]`, not bundled, `license_review.status: pending` (Gemma Terms), `input_modalities: [text]` (no mmproj — image translation out of scope), `recommended_context_tokens: 4096` (the sidecar `--ctx-size`, not a chat window), `recommended_min_ram_gb: 14` (placeholder, TG-2 remeasures). **V2–V4 re-verified 2026-07-05 (unauthenticated HF API):** URL fetchable (resolve → 302 to a PUBLIC xet CDN, `X-Xet-Cas-Uid=public` — google base repo is gated, the mradermacher GGUF repo is not); exact filename `translategemma-12b-it.Q4_K_M.gguf`; `size_bytes 7300794112` (tree `size` == `X-Linked-Size`); **sha256 `b7aac4b4…a528`** = the file's git-LFS OID cross-checked against `X-Linked-ETag` (never transcribed uncomputed — the qwen3.5-9b precedent). **ModelsScreen:** `translation` added to the `automatic` role list + `plainHintKey` + a translation card-hint (installed/notInstalled), i18n keys in BOTH en.ts + de.ts (parity compile-enforced). **Docs:** model-policy.md new "The translation role + TranslateGemma" §ection (license-review record + the `--jinja` #20305 regression research note + the plain-Gemma-3/no-pin-bump fact); README supporting-models row; user-guide download-with-license-prompt note. **Tests:** manifest validator (accepts translation role + download block, still rejects unknown role); models integration (translation install-state + selectModel refusal); drive layout dir assertions; two ModelsScreen renderer cards (missing → Download + "used automatically for translation"; installed → no Select/Start). `npm test` **3365 pass / 44 skip**, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build` all green. **Exit met:** the model appears on the AI Model screen, is downloadable behind the license-ack checkbox, shows `installed` after hash verify; NO other behavior changed. **Next: TG-2** — `services/translation/` sidecar (`--ctx-size 4096 --parallel 1`, no `--jinja`, raw `/completion`) + the real-pin b9849 load smoke (the go/no-go gate)._

_2026-07-05 — **Result tables Phase 3 v1 — TableRequest parse + derived-column enrichment — on `master` (merged via PR #16).** (Working paper: `docs/result-tables-plan.md` §5, v1 implemented; no-skill routing consciously DEFERRED there.)
"Zeig den Kontoauszug als CSV mit einer Spalte Empfänger" now works: user-requested EXTRA columns are parsed grammar-constrained and filled per row over the whole statement. **How (NEW `services/skills/enricher.ts`):** (1) `wantsExtraColumns` — deterministic PRE-GATE (spalte/column/subcategor/payee/empfänger… stems): a plain "als CSV" stays the 0-model short-circuit (pinned by a zero-calls test); only a column-shaped ask pays the parse. (2) `parseTableRequest` — ONE D55 grammar-constrained call → `DerivedColumn {name, description?, enumValues?}[]` (≤4, names ≤40); validation ALL-OR-NOTHING (D65): invalid/fixed-shadowing (`Betrag`/`category`/…) names reject the whole request → plain-table fallback, never half-understood; empty list = valid no-op. (3) `enrichRows` — batched 12 rows/call, ONE call fills ALL requested columns per batch (`{assignments:[{index, values:{col: …}}]}`, additionalProperties:false, required per column); enum columns grammar-enum-constrained + the `unknown` drop target; free text length-capped 60; `unknown`/invalid/dropped → **blank cell** (absent, never invented); unparseable → one retry → blanks; runaway char-cap (the categorizer's L-1/L-2 posture); whole-statement by construction (rows = the deterministic extract). (4) Bank handler CSV format path: enriched turns emit an EXTENDED `TableSpec` (fixed + presence-gated category + derived) through the fenced answer AND the Phase-2 result table (→ the message-level export carries the derived columns); honest `skills.bankAnalysis.derivedColumnsNote` (EN+DE) names the model-filled columns under the fence; any parse/enrich fault falls back to the plain table. **Data contracts:** `DerivedColumn`, `ENRICH_UNKNOWN`, `MAX_DERIVED_COLUMNS=4`, `ENRICH_BATCH_SIZE=12`; derived values are NOT persisted on `bank_transactions` (per-answer only, via `result_tables` — re-asking re-pays the fill). Tests: NEW `tests/unit/enricher.test.ts` (9 — pre-gate, parse validate/all-or-nothing/empty-valid, enum+unknown schema, blank-on-unknown/off-enum, retry-then-blanks, batching with global index mapping), +2 bank integration (end-to-end parse→enrich→8+1-column CSV + note + extended result table, keyed off `responseSchemaName`; pre-gate zero-calls + offline plain-table fallback). Suite 3413 pass (same 2 pre-existing benchmark failures — untracked draft manifests); typecheck + build green. **Deferred/residuals (recorded in plan §5):** no-skill tabular routing needs a GENERIC row extractor (doesn't exist; A4 inversion covers most statement asks in practice); the model parse GATES on the regex pre-gate rather than replacing the format regexes (cost-honest); `filter`/`groupBy`/`deliverable` fields unparsed; JSON asks not enriched (CSV only); invoice `TableSpec` port still open (§6)._

_2026-07-05 — **Result tables Phase 2 — per-message result-table artifact + message-level "Export CSV" — on `master` (merged via PR #16)** (stacked on PR #14, merged just below). (Working paper: `docs/result-tables-plan.md` §4, now IMPLEMENTED.)
Closes the chat→file half of the original "Kategorisiere … und exportiere als CSV" ask: a bank format answer now ends in a SAVE DIALOG, not just a copyable code block. **How:** (1) NEW `result_tables` (db.ts SCHEMA): one generic tabular artifact per assistant message — columns/rows JSON (CONTENT, never logged), row_count, content-free `source`, `message_id` FK **ON DELETE CASCADE** (regenerate delete + conversation delete purge automatically; no doc-purge hook needed — tables key off messages). Store seam `services/tables/store.ts`: `saveResultTable` best-effort (empty / over `MAX_RESULT_TABLE_ROWS` 10 000 / unserializable → persists nothing, the ANSWER is never blocked — the `serializeCoverage` posture), `loadResultTable` tolerant like `parseCoverage`. (2) `SkillAnalysisResult.table?: TableSpec` — the bank format path (CSV and JSON asks) emits `transactionsTableSpec(rows)` (NEW: the one column definition `transactionsToCsv` now delegates to); `registerRagIpc` persists it right after the plain-answer `appendMessage` and sets `Message.hasResultTable` on the returned object (renderer sees it without reload). `listMessages` derives the flag via an EXISTS subselect — table content never rides message loading. (3) NEW IPC `chat:exportMessageTable` (registerChatIpc, the `exportConversation` template): requireUnlocked → `loadResultTable` → `tableToCsv` (the ONE audited CSV path, formula-injection neutralization included) → `saveTextExport` — the save dialog IS the consent (no confirm modal; same posture as the transcript export; no skill tool runs; no BOM on .csv). Audit `message_table_exported`: `{ messageId, rows }` ONLY. (4) Renderer: "Export CSV" in `MessageActions`, rendered only on `hasResultTable` answers, threaded Transcript → ChatScreen → `window.api.exportMessageTable`, success = the existing "saved to …" toast; i18n `chat.actions.exportCsv(+Title)` / `main.dialog.exportTableCsv` / `diag.audit.message_table_exported` (EN+DE). **Data contracts:** `result_tables` schema above; `Message.hasResultTable?: boolean` (positive-flag convention, absent elsewhere); `SkillAnalysisResult.table?: TableSpec<object>`. Tests: NEW `tests/integration/result-tables.test.ts` (6 — store round-trip + caps, listMessages flag, cascade purge, export IPC write incl. dialog metadata + content-free audit sentinel, cancel, no-table-no-dialog), bank integration asserts the emitted 8-column table, NEW renderer cases in `TranscriptA11y.test.tsx` (button only on hasResultTable + wired to the right message id; absent without the handler). Suite 3402 pass (same 2 pre-existing benchmark failures — untracked draft manifests); typecheck + build green. **Residuals:** only the bank FORMAT path emits a table (invoice port + template-path tables are follow-ups); pre-Phase-2 answers carry no table (no backfill, by design); Phase 3 (TableRequest + enrichment + no-skill tabular routing) remains open._

_2026-07-05 — **Result tables Phase 1.6 — taxonomy CSV referenced from the prompt — on `master` (merged via PR #14).** (Working paper: `docs/result-tables-plan.md` §3b, D66–D67.)
Follow-up to Phase 1.5 (below): the category set can now live in an IMPORTED CSV — "Kategorisiere nach den Kategorien in `taxonomie.csv` und gib alles als CSV aus" works end-to-end. **How:** (1) `parseTaxonomyFileRef` (categorizer.ts): categorize stem + a quoted-or-bare `.csv` token (quotes allow spaces). (2) `findDocumentByName` (analysis/bank-statement.ts, D66): case-insensitive title lookup across the INDEXED library (extension-stripped stems match; statement excluded; ties → most recently updated) — a LOOKUP, never a scope widening, so the handler's single-in-scope-doc contract is untouched. (3) `readDocumentPlainText`: faithful parser segments when the IPC injected the reader, chunks-table fallback in tests. (4) `parseTaxonomyCsv` (D65-consistent all-or-nothing): one label per line, delimiter = first of `;`/tab/`,`, header row + `#`-comments skipped, 2–40 labels, dedupe; the REMAINING cells become the label's **GLOSS** (D67) — `Kinder;Schule, Kita, Taschengeld` renders `- Kinder (Schule, Kita, Taschengeld)` in the categorizer system prompt (the same mechanism the fixed taxonomy's DE glosses use; never persisted, never an enum value). New `CustomCategory {name, gloss?}` / `CustomCategoryInput` types; `categorizeTransactions`/`batchOutputSchema`/`buildSystemPrompt` accept bare strings (Phase 1.5 inline, unchanged) or label+gloss objects. (5) Missing file / unparseable list → honest refusals NAMING the file (`skills.bankAnalysis.customTaxonomyNotFound` / `.customTaxonomyUnparseable`, EN+DE); the parsed set then rides the exact Phase-1.5 path (covered-check reuse, no-runtime refusal, inline categorize in the chat slot, `persistCategorization`). Tests: +8 categorizer units (file-ref quoted/bare/no-stem; CSV gloss/header/comments/all-or-nothing/dedupe; gloss-reaches-prompt + names-only enum), +2 bank integration (library taxonomy → custom-label CSV with the gloss asserted IN the model prompt; missing + prose-file refusals). Suite 3389 pass (same 2 pre-existing benchmark failures — untracked draft manifests); typecheck + build green. **Amendment (same day, first real use):** a FULL-PATH reference (`'/home/…/HVB/taxonomie.csv'`) is now reduced to its BASENAME before the library lookup (titles carry no path; the path is also never echoed back — content minimization), and FILE labels accept the real-world shapes `Kfz/Auto` / `Essen & Trinken` / `Vers. + Vorsorge` via `TAXONOMY_LABEL_RE` (wider than the inline `LABEL_RE`, where `/`,`+`,`.` signal a swallowed clause). +4 units. The app still deliberately cannot READ an arbitrary disk path — the CSV must be imported first (§14 capability ceiling); the refusal copy says so. **Amendment 2 (same day, second real use — "couldn’t use it as a category list"):** the taxonomy parser was reading the CSV IMPORTER's stored text, not the raw file — `ingestion/parsers/csv.ts` linearizes a header-ful CSV into `Header: value; Header2: value2` lines (and the comma-pinned `.csv` import additionally mangles a German semicolon-CSV: cells collapse into the first value, overflow becomes `colN:` pairs), so EVERY imported header-ful taxonomy failed the parse. `parseTaxonomyCsv` now detects the linearized shape (constant first pair key across all lines) and RECONSTRUCTS the original rows — keys → the header row (re-emitted so header-skip applies; `colN` artefacts dropped; a headerless file's importer-eaten first row is recovered), values → `;`-rejoined rows — before the normal label/gloss split; raw text (pasted lists, the test chunk path) passes through untouched. +3 units pinning the three ingested shapes (comma-CSV, mangled semicolon-CSV, headerless). Residual: the importer's linearization itself (RAG-N5 pinned `.csv` to comma) still degrades semicolon-CSVs for RETRIEVAL generally — a `;` delimiter sniff at import is a separate ingestion follow-up. **Residuals:** with NO explicit scope the chat filename auto-scope can narrow the turn to the taxonomy file itself (handler falls through to relevance; select the statement when referencing a taxonomy — documented in known-limitations); taxonomy files larger than one chunk risk a boundary-split label on the chunk fallback path (irrelevant ≤ 40 labels); a persistent default taxonomy (settings) remains the Phase-2/3 candidate._

_2026-07-05 — **Result tables Phase 1.5 — user-defined category sets from the prompt — on `master` (merged via PR #14).** (Working paper: `docs/result-tables-plan.md` §3a, D64–D65.)
Follow-up to Phase 1 (below): the rule pass is coarse (Income/Spending/Transfer) and the fixed 15-label model taxonomy may not match the user's buckets — "Kategorisiere in Miete, Lebensmittel, Kinder und Sonstiges und exportiere als CSV" now works end-to-end in ONE chat turn. **How:** (1) `categorizer.ts` takes an optional per-run `categories` list — the grammar enum (`batchOutputSchema`), the system prompt (`buildSystemPrompt`; user labels verbatim, no gloss), and the reply validation all use the custom set + an always-appended `Uncategorized` drop target; the confident deterministic PRE-FILTER is SKIPPED on a custom set (its rule names are the fixed taxonomy's, not the user's). NEW `parseRequestedCategories` (D65): conservative, all-or-nothing prompt parse — categorize stem + preposition (EN+DE), deliverable-tail cut ("… und exportiere als CSV" never becomes a label), ≥2 plausible labels (≤40 chars, ≤4 words each), dedupe, ONE bad token rejects the whole parse (never silently categorize into garbage); `csv`/`json`/`Uncategorized` are never labels. (2) The bank analysis handler runs the categorizer **INLINE in the chat slot** (D64 — the turn already holds the exclusive model slot, the same one grounded-data streams in; no doctask enqueue, no second lane at the llama-server), REUSING a prior run when the persisted labels ⊆ the requested set (asking for the CSV again is free); with **no runtime** the ask is REFUSED with the new `skills.bankAnalysis.customCategoriesNeedModel` copy (EN+DE) that ECHOES the parsed set — a silent fixed-taxonomy fallback would answer a different question. (3) NEW `persistCategorization` seam (run.ts): atomic assignments + `categorized_by_model` persist, user labels inserted as NON-builtin `bank_categories` rows, looked up across ALL rows (no UNIQUE on name — a prior custom run's row is reused, never duplicated). (4) `SkillAnalysisContext.runtime` (types.ts) is the one sanctioned model hook, threaded from registerRagIpc. (5) **Privacy fix found by the wave:** `categoryLabel` probed the i18n catalog with the raw category name and the catalog LOGS unknown keys — a user-defined category name (content-class) would have leaked into the diagnostics log; it now probes only `LOCALIZED_CATEGORY_NAMES` (the fixed taxonomies) and shows a custom name verbatim (§22-M1). **Data contracts:** `categorizeTransactions(deps.categories?)`; `persistCategorization(db, statementId, loaded, assignments, modelAssisted, now?)`; custom `bank_categories` rows have `builtin=0`. Tests: +8 categorizer units (parse EN/DE/tail-cut/reject/dedupe; custom enum + prompt + off-set drop + no-runtime Uncategorized), +2 bank integration (no-runtime refusal echoing the set; scripted-runtime inline categorize → custom-label CSV + assisted note + REUSE with zero extra model calls). Full suite 3379 pass (same 2 pre-existing benchmark failures from the owner's untracked draft manifests); typecheck + build green. **Residuals:** model REQUIRED for custom sets (honest refusal without); no per-batch progress meter inside the chat turn (the ephemeral analysis notice covers it; the button lane has one); custom sets are per-ask, not saved (a persistent user taxonomy in settings is a Phase-2/3 candidate); the inline categorize bypasses the `skill_runs` lifecycle row (the doctask/button lanes keep theirs — a chat-lane run row is a follow-up)._

_2026-07-05 — **Result tables Phase 1 — categorize + CSV/JSON combined, category column end-to-end — on `master` (merged via PR #14).** (Working paper: `docs/result-tables-plan.md`, D59–D63; Phases 2–3 open.)
Fixes the observed failure "Kategorisiere alle Transaktionen und exportiere als CSV": the bank format short-circuit returned on the `csv` token BEFORE the categorization block, and neither CSV surface could carry a category (`transactionsToCsv` hardcoded 7 columns; `run.ts loadTransactions` never joined `bank_categories`). **What changed:** (1) NEW generic, pure `services/tables/` — `TableSpec` (columns as DATA: key/label/kind text|money|integer) + `tableToCsv` (one audited serializer: `csvField` neutralization on text cells AND labels, fixed 2-dp money, `\r\n` + trailing newline) — D59/D60. (2) `TransactionInput.category?` (+ `TRANSACTION_ROW_SCHEMA` + the committed `transaction.schema.json`): the persisted category name rides WITH the row through the existing seams (`preloaded`, tool input, export) — no index-matched parallel array crosses a seam (D61). (3) `transactionsToCsv` builds a `TableSpec` and delegates; the `category` column (and the JSON `category` field in `buildStatementJson`) is **presence-gated** — emitted only when ≥1 row carries a category, so every never-categorized flow stays BYTE-IDENTICAL (D62; the existing 7-column pins still pass unchanged). (4) `analysis/bank-statement.ts`: the category block (seed-if-unpersisted via `runCategorization` + reload + `modelAssisted`) is HOISTED above the format short-circuit; a category-shaped format ask serializes rows WITH categories (`p.category ?? categorizeRow(p.row)`) and `buildFormatAnswer` appends the honest `categoryAssisted`/`categoryRuleBased` note under the fenced block (D63 — a category is a label, never a parser figure; no new i18n keys). `export_transactions_csv` still NEVER auto-runs from chat. (5) `run.ts loadTransactions` LEFT JOINs `bank_categories` → the confirm-gated file export emits the category column whenever the statement was categorized (button or chat lane). **Data contracts:** `TransactionInput.category?: string` (optional everywhere; extractor never emits it); `TableSpec`/`tableToCsv` in `services/tables`. Tests: NEW `tests/unit/tables.test.ts` (5 — generic contract incl. formula-shaped labels/cells); unit CSV presence-gate + category-neutralization + JSON-gate tests; integration `skills-analysis-bank.test.ts` flagship regression ("Kategorisiere … als CSV" → categorize seam RAN + 8-column CSV + rule-based note + still 0-model short-circuit; JSON parity; plain "as CSV" pinned category-less). `npm test`: 3368 pass / 41 skip — the ONLY 2 failures are `benchmark.test.ts` recommendation picks caused by the owner's UNTRACKED draft model manifests in `model-manifests/chat/` (gemma-4-26b/qwen3.5-*; verified: suite passes 27/27 with them moved aside — pre-existing local state, not this wave). `typecheck` green. **Next:** Phase 2 (persistent `result_tables` artifact + message-level confirm-gated export affordance — closes "export" from chat), Phase 3 (grammar-constrained `TableRequest` parse + per-row derived-column enrichment + routing no-skill tabular asks off top-k), invoice-handler `TableSpec` port. **Residuals:** chat cannot write a file (by design until Phase 2); columns fixed to the transaction shape + category until Phase 3._

_2026-07-05 — **Whole-doc analysis truncation — follow-ups #1 + #2 (grounded-path continuation + hierarchical fold) — on `master`.** (Design record: `docs/rag-design.md` §14.10; the truncation-fix plan was already retired at Phase 4.)
Two owner-requested follow-ups to the retired wave. **#1 — continue-generation on the single-turn grounded path.** Phase 4's continuation loop was extracted from `streamWholeDocMapReduce` into a SHARED engine **`continueUntilComplete`** ([rag/whole-doc-tree.ts]) that takes the whole prior message array (system + history + the grounded/reduce USER turn) and appends a resume instruction + anchor to its LAST user turn (`withContinuation`), streaming the seam-deduped remainder (`seamOverlap`) into a mutable accumulator; it handles its own Stop mid-continuation (flush + return null) and returns the final finish reason. The reduce core now calls it (behavior byte-identical — all mapreduce/tree tests unchanged). `generateGroundedAnswer` now finishes a `finishReason==='length'`-cut grounded answer (relevance top-k, small-doc fits-budget read, whole-doc capped read) across bounded re-prompts instead of persisting a mid-word partial; the per-pass room guard sizes each continuation against the ACTUAL assembled prompt so history + the grounded block never overflow `n_ctx`. **#2 — hierarchical fold for the large-document tail.** The map-call ceiling used to drop the tail past `SUMMARY_MAP_CALL_CEILING` (~12 windows ≈ ~50 pages). Now up to **`SUMMARY_MAP_CALL_HARD_CEILING`** (= ×2, ~100 pages) windows are mapped, and beyond the single-level ceiling the per-window notes are CONDENSED down through bounded fenced intermediate reduces (`foldUserPrompt`, cap `MAX_FOLD_DEPTH`) until they fit one final reduce — so the WHOLE document (to the hard ceiling) is covered (`truncated:false`). ≤ ceiling path byte-identical (single-level join → reduce). Beyond the hard ceiling: honestly beginning-only (`truncated:true`, deep-index tree territory — the tree auto-builds at ~this size; the fold is a deliberately-bounded query-time lever, dominant cost = one map call per window on CPU, covered by the Phase 3 notice). **Data contracts:** the `Message.truncated` (OUTPUT) vs `coverage.truncated` (INPUT) split from Phase 4 is unchanged; #2 keeps the INPUT-coverage stamp honest at the raised ceiling. Tests: `rag.test.ts` (#1 grounded happy-path finish + bounded/seam-deduped exhaustion via a scripted runtime; existing grounded-'length' test still green — an always-cut runtime now dedups to the same content + stamps truncated); `rag-whole-doc-mapreduce.test.ts` (#2 fold-coverage at 350 sentences/ctx 2048 → 16 windows folded whole, and hard-ceiling at 1000 → sliced + last marker dropped); `rag-whole-doc-tree.test.ts` ceiling test re-pointed to `SUMMARY_MAP_CALL_HARD_CEILING`. `npm test` (3357 pass / 44 skip) + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. Commits: `feat(rag): extend continue-generation to the single-turn grounded path (follow-up #1)` + `feat(rag): hierarchical fold for the large-document tail (follow-up #2)`. **Residuals:** beyond ~100 pages still beginning-only (tree territory); a deliverable still cut after the 2-continuation cap keeps the honest OUTPUT-truncated badge._

_2026-07-05 — **Whole-doc analysis truncation — Phase 4: continue-generation for over-cap deliverables (FINAL phase; plan retired) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/wholedoc-truncation-fix-plan.md` §6 — this was the last phase; the plan is being folded into `docs/rag-design.md` + `docs/architecture.md` §19/§20 in a separate docs-fold commit, then deleted per the CLAUDE.md doc-lifecycle rule.)
Diagnosis (residual (c)): on a small (4 k) window a very long deliverable that Phase 2 caps to fit is still cut at the output ceiling (`finishReason === 'length'`) and persisted mid-sentence; the reduce previously **ignored its finish reason entirely**, so an over-cap answer carried NO output-truncated signal at all. Fix, in the SHARED core `streamWholeDocMapReduce` ([rag/whole-doc-tree.ts]) — so BOTH the tree rescue and the chunk map-reduce (and the single-window reduce) get it: the reduce stream now captures `finishReason` via `onFinish` (mirroring the single-turn grounded path in [rag/index.ts]); when it ends `'length'` (a ceiling cut — NOT a user Stop, which fires no finish reason), a **continuation loop** re-prompts to FINISH the deliverable instead of persisting a mid-sentence answer. **What each pass does + when:** re-sends the SAME reduce USER turn (fence + notes + question + `extraReduceBlock` — the §2 "fence at every step" invariant, full grounding preserved) via new `continuationUserPrompt(reduceUser, anchor)` that adds a short resume instruction ("Continue the answer exactly from where it stops — do NOT repeat…") + an `anchor` = the last `CONTINUATION_ANCHOR_CHARS` (200) chars produced; streams live via `onToken`, holding back only the opening until the seam overlap against the anchor is resolved (`seamOverlap` — the longest anchor-tail↔continuation-head match, ≤ anchor length) then emits the DE-DUPLICATED remainder (a re-emitted anchor is never doubled). **Bounds:** `MAX_REDUCE_CONTINUATIONS = 2` (runaway guard) AND a per-pass no-overflow room guard — the continuation prompt is LARGER than the reduce prompt, so its `maxTokens = min(reduceOutputCap, contextTokens − continuePromptTokens)` is sized against the ACTUAL assembled prompt and the loop stops (`CONTINUATION_MIN_OUTPUT_TOKENS = 256` floor) rather than assemble a prompt the runtime would reject (HTTP 400 "exceeds context size", invariant §2). All INSIDE the existing try/catch, so a Stop mid-continuation is caught and the accumulated partial persisted (the aborted pass's partial folded in via a `finally` seam-flush) — never a fresh pass past the abort. **Stamp decision (data contract):** `Message.truncated = true` is stamped ONLY when continue-generation is EXHAUSTED (the 2-cap or room guard hit and the last pass is still `'length'`) — an honest OUTPUT-truncation badge ("Answer truncated — model context limit reached"), **parity with the single-turn grounded path's `messages.truncated`**. It is kept STRICTLY separate from `coverage.truncated` (INPUT coverage — "covers the beginning"): the whole document can be covered (`coverage.truncated:false`) while the deliverable is output-cut (`Message.truncated:true`); a user Stop leaves it false (intentional, not an overflow). **Scope:** the shared reduce core only; the single-turn small-doc fits-budget read was left UNCHANGED (ample output room ⇒ minor residual — a documented follow-up, per §6's OPTIONAL marking). Tests: a new `finishScriptingRuntime` in `rag-whole-doc-mapreduce.test.ts` (scripts a finish reason + reply per call and FIRES `options.onFinish` — the signal the Phase 1–3 recording runtimes never send, so those cases still never continue) drives 5 cases: happy path (length→continuation→stop = concatenation, seam de-duplicated, >1 call, no stamp), seam-overlap dedup (marker once), hard cap (`1 + MAX_REDUCE_CONTINUATIONS` calls then `Message.truncated:true` while `coverage.truncated` stays false), Stop mid-continuation (partial persisted, no further pass), first-pass-stop (zero continuations). `npm test` (3353 pass / 44 skip) + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Next actions:** docs-fold commit (retire the plan file); optionally extend continue-generation to the single-turn grounded path if 4 k small-doc cuts are seen in practice. **Residuals:** a deliverable long enough to STILL be cut after the 2-continuation cap keeps the honest OUTPUT-truncated badge; ≥~50-page tail still beginning-only (map-call ceiling, INPUT coverage)._

_2026-07-05 — **Whole-doc analysis truncation — Phase 3: analysis progress affordance — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/wholedoc-truncation-fix-plan.md` §5; Phase 4 optional, still open.)
Diagnosis (residual (b)): the whole-doc map-reduce runs SILENT map calls before the first streamed reduce token (2–12 model calls on a mid-size doc), which reads as a hang. Fix: fire the existing ephemeral `'analysis'` progress notice ("Reading the whole document…") in the SHARED core `streamWholeDocMapReduce` ([rag/whole-doc-tree.ts]) — so BOTH the tree rescue and the chunk map-reduce path get it (consistent with Phase 1/2). **What fires it + when:** `onCompactionStart?.('analysis')`, fired ONLY when `windows.length > 1` (a real map loop), placed AFTER the `if (answerPrefix) onToken?.(answerPrefix)` line and immediately BEFORE the map loop — so it is visible for exactly the silent map-call window and the renderer clears it on the first reduce token. A single-window (reduce-only) turn, the fits-budget single read, and the needle/relevance paths never run silent map calls and fire NO notice. **Wiring:** `onCompactionStart?: (kind: 'analysis') => void` threaded through `WholeDocMapReduceInput` + `WholeDocTreeDeps` + `WholeDocChunksDeps` and both whole-doc calls in the `opts.wholeDocument` branch; `GroundedAnswerOptions.onCompactionStart` widened `() => void` → `(kind?: 'analysis') => void`. The grounded-whole-doc IPC path already passes `sendCompaction` (sends `{phase:'start', kind}`) as `onCompactionStart`, so **NO IPC change** and **NO new `CompactionNotice` kind** — the `'analysis'` kind + `chat.analysis.inProgress` copy (EN "Reading the whole document…" / DE "Das ganze Dokument wird gelesen…") were reused as-is (forbidden-word-safe; `shared/ipc.ts`/`chat-stream.ts`/`Transcript.tsx`/`ChatScreen.tsx` untouched). **Invariants kept:** ephemeral (R14 — never buffered/persisted); SEC-1 (a callback — no new handle); Stop/abort, needle-downgrade, relevance paths byte-unchanged. Tests: renderer `ChatCompaction.test.tsx` (whole-doc analysis turn shows the notice + clears on first reduce token); integration `rag-whole-doc-mapreduce.test.ts` (a recording `onCompactionStart` through `generateGroundedAnswer` is called exactly once with `'analysis'` on a 280-sentence multi-window turn, not at all on a 90-sentence single-window turn). `npm test` (3348 pass / 44 skip) + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Next actions:** Phase 4 optional (continue-generation for complete deliverables at 4 k). **Residuals:** ≥~50-page tail still beginning-only (map-call ceiling); 4 k-window output cut until Phase 4 — but the latency gap (b) now carries a progress affordance._

_2026-07-04 — **Whole-doc analysis truncation — Phase 2: adaptive reduce output reserve (no `n_ctx` overflow) — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/wholedoc-truncation-fix-plan.md` §4; Phases 3–4 still open.)
Diagnosis (root cause #2): the map-reduce **reduce** step reserved a fixed `CHAT_RESPONSE_RESERVE_TOKENS` (1024) for output, so a long structured deliverable (a 9-section contract brief) ran into `n_ctx` and was stamped `truncated`. Fix: **`ANALYSIS_RESPONSE_RESERVE_TOKENS = 3072`** added in [chat.ts] (the *desired* reduce output; `CHAT_…` stays the floor) + a new pure, unit-tested helper **`computeReduceBudget({contextTokens, fenceTokens, questionTokens, notesTokens})`** in [rag/whole-doc-tree.ts] that sizes `reduceOutputCap` (→ `maxTokens`) and `reduceNotesBudget` (→ the notes hard-cut, replacing the old `budgetWords` cut) from the REAL launched context. It runs inside the shared `streamWholeDocMapReduce`, so **BOTH the tree and chunk paths** get the adaptive budget. **Owner-approved policy — NOTES-FIRST** (deliberate deviation from the plan's original output-first clamp, decided 2026-07-04 via an in-task question): the output reserve **yields** to the actual notes — it aims for 3072 but shrinks toward 1024 (never below) so a small (4 k) window keeps **whole-document coverage** and the *deliverable* shrinks instead; the notes are hard-truncated (⇒ `truncated:true`) only when even the floor output leaves no room. This preserves Phase 1's gap-band closure at the default 4 k (the plan's literal output-first formula would have reopened it by reserving more output than the single read it replaces). With a realistic ~980-token fence+question and notes that fit, the numbers still match the plan's worked examples (4096 → cap 2476, notes 512; 8192 → cap 3072). **Data contract — reduce budget (model tokens):** `overhead = fenceTokens + questionTokens + 128`; `available = ctx − overhead`; `reduceOutputCap = clamp(ANALYSIS=3072 aimed, floor CHAT=1024, available − max(notesTokens, 512))`; `reduceNotesBudget = max(512, available − reduceOutputCap)`; guarantee `overhead + reduceNotesBudget + reduceOutputCap ≤ ctx` at every real window (≥ 2 048). Notes cut converts model→word units via `SUMMARY_TOKENS_PER_WORD`. **`wholeDocumentFitBudgetTokens` UNCHANGED** (the single-turn input-budget + needle-downgrade boundary; the capped/fit grounded path keeps `CHAT_…`). Tests: new `tests/unit/wholedoc-reduce-budget.test.ts` (budget math at ctx 2048/4096/8192/32768 + worked examples) + two cases in `rag-whole-doc-mapreduce.test.ts` (ctx 8192 full reserve/untruncated; ctx 4096 floored + honest badge, asserting `maxTokens === reduceOutputCap`). Tree + Phase-1 chunk tests unchanged (notes-first preserved their outcomes byte-identically). `npm test` (3345 pass) + `npm run typecheck` + `npm run build` green. **Next actions:** Phase 3 (progress affordance — `sendCompaction('analysis')` before the map loop + en/de copy); Phase 4 optional (continue-generation for complete deliverables at 4 k). **Residual:** a 4 k window still output-cuts a very long brief (deliverable shrinks; coverage preserved) until Phase 4._

_2026-07-04 — **Whole-doc analysis truncation — Phase 1: chunk map-reduce closes the mid-size "gap band" — on `master`.** (Plan: `docs/wholedoc-truncation-fix-plan.md` §3; Phase 2 now done, Phases 3–4 still open.)
Diagnosis: an over-budget `analysis: whole-doc` turn (e.g. a `contract-brief` over a multi-page PDF) with NO deep-index tree was read from the **beginning only** — the tree rescue (`answerWholeDocFromTree`) auto-builds far too late (~50 pages), so every document between ~1.5 and ~50 pages truncated to the beginning and never got a tree. Fix: **extracted `streamWholeDocMapReduce(input)`** — the shared fence→pack→map→reduce→stream→persist core — from `answerWholeDocFromTree` ([rag/whole-doc-tree.ts]); the tree path is now a thin pre-model gate that calls it with `coverageMode:'tree'` (**byte-identical**, pinned by `rag-whole-doc-tree.test.ts`). New **`answerWholeDocFromChunks(deps)`** in [rag/index.ts] runs an on-the-fly map-reduce over the document's RAW chunks (`coverageMode:'capped'`) and is wired as `viaTree ?? viaChunks ?? (capped floor)` in the `opts.wholeDocument` branch. The de-overlap read is factored into a private **`readWholeDocumentChunkTexts`** (single home; `retrieveWholeDocument` now consumes it too — byte-identical). Citations = a bounded representative sample of REAL leaf chunks (≤ `SUMMARY_MAP_CALL_CEILING`, M2). **Share-safe parity:** the chunk path passes `buildShareSafeScanBlock(scan, false)` as `extraReduceBlock` (reduce USER turn, never system) — whole-doc coverage legitimately permits the low-risk verdict. **Coverage stamp (data contract):** `mode:'capped', truncated:false, chunksCovered===chunksTotal` = whole-doc via map-reduce (the meter's existing "covers the whole document" meaning). `truncated:true` only on a > ceiling window count or a notes hard-cut. **Untouched (Phase 2):** the reduce output reserve stays `CHAT_RESPONSE_RESERVE_TOKENS`. Tests: new `rag-whole-doc-mapreduce.test.ts` (whole-doc reach — first+last `M####` markers in the map inputs; >1 call; one-window→single reduce; Stop-before-first-token→empty message, no 2nd pass; share-safe block placement); re-pointed the old beginning-only defect assertions in `rag-whole-doc-truncation.test.ts` + `rag-whole-doc-skill.test.ts`. **Verification:** `npm test` **3331 passed / 44 skipped**; typecheck clean; build OK. **Next actions:** Phase 2 (adaptive reduce output reserve — no `n_ctx` overflow), Phase 3 (progress notice), Phase 4 (optional continue-generation). **Residuals:** ≥~50-page tail still beginning-only (map-call ceiling); 2–12 model calls of latency on mid-size analysis; 4 k-window output cut until Phase 2/4._

_2026-07-04 — **Invoice hardening wave (user-reported bad conversation: JSON replay, garbage totals, unanswerable Empfänger, export mojibake) — P1–P4 on stacked `fix/invoice-hardening-p1…p4`, merged to `master`.** Triggered by a real exported transcript (glyph-mangled crypto-scam invoice PDF): (1) "…im Roh format - nicht im json" re-served the byte-identical JSON dump in 9 ms (bare `\bjson\b` matched inside a negation; deterministic 0-model-call path); (2) confident garbage totals (net 4 / tax 0 / gross 914 vs items ~1084) printed under "exactly as printed" and echoed as "wörtlich aus dem Dokument" although `validateInvoiceTotals` had detected the mismatch; (3) "Wer ist der Empfänger?" structurally unanswerable (no recipient field; grounded-data confines the model to the extract); (4) exported .md rendered mojibake in CP1252 viewers (no BOM). Fixes: **P1** negation-/raw-aware `detectFormat` (per-mention 24-char negator window; Rohformat/raw/Fließtext… disables the short-circuit) + a conversation-level byte-identical-replay backstop. **P2** (extractor v10) weak bare-integer totals are corroboration-gated (`dropUncorroboratedWeakTotals`); answer layers mirror the bank `contradicted` suppression (UNVERIFIED totals heading + caveat, figure-echo suppression note, data-block WARNING). **P3** (extractor v11) `looksLikeGlyphSoup` → `invoices.text_quality` 'suspect' → ONE geometry (layout) re-extraction → `'suspect-confirmed'` final; on a suspect layer figures present only when POSITIVELY reconciled, else the localized `unreadableLayout` refusal; header gains `recipient` (labeled lines only, `invoices.recipient`); a question naming a missing header field falls through to the relevance path. **P4** corpus gains `invoice-us-glyph-soup`, `invoice-de-unreconcilable-totals`, and the FIRST geometry-invoice fixture `invoice-de-geometry-columns` (real `reconstructPage`; closes the T1 plain-text-only residual), snapshot regenerated (v11); `.md`/`.txt` exports get a UTF-8 BOM (`bomFor`, never JSON/log). New i18n keys (en+de): totalsHeadingUnverified, unreconciledCaveat, figureEchoSuppressed, detailRecipient, unreadableLayout, textQualityCaveat. New nullable `invoices.recipient` + `invoices.text_quality` columns (additive `ensureColumn`). Design record: **architecture.md "Skills — design record" §42**; residuals: known-limitations.md (glyph-soup bullet). Full suite green at each phase._

_2026-07-04 — **Context truth end-to-end (user report "meter 7% but context full"): real-usage meter + grounded truncation parity + no cap-mislabel + one window everywhere + user context-size picker — on `master`.**
Triggered by a user report (5-page PDF "hit the context limit" while the composer meter read 7%; asked whether context differs per area / is per-chat; requested a UI context-size option). All four observations were real seams; full design record **rag-design §15.8** (architecture.md "Chat & streaming" bullets + model-policy.md amended). **(1) Meter honesty:** the generators (`generateAssistantMessage` / `generateGroundedAnswer` / `generateGroundedDataAnswer`) gain `onPromptUsage` — fired once post-assembly with the REAL assembled prompt's `messageTokens` sum over the launched window; `withChatStream` gains a 5th runFn sender `sendUsage` → new EPHEMERAL `STREAM.usage(convId)` channel (R14 posture, never buffered) → preload `onContextUsage` → `ChatScreen.streamUsage` replaces the estimate base while that conversation streams (answer estimate rides on top; `liveUserTokens` ignored — the report already carries the user turn), cleared in the stream `finally` so the meter reconciles to the resting read (correct: the excerpt block is per-turn). A documents turn now shows the window genuinely filling instead of single digits. **(2) Grounded truncation parity (§L0):** both grounded generators pass `onFinish` and stamp `messages.truncated` on `finish_reason 'length'` — the doc path is where the ceiling actually hits; was plain-chat-only. **(3) Cap ≠ context limit:** `generateAssistantMessage` flags truncated ONLY with no `max_tokens` cap in effect (`runtimeOptions.maxTokens ?? requestParamsForMode(mode).maxTokens == null`) — a Fast-mode (1024-cap) reply wearing the "reached the model's context limit" badge at 7% was itself a false "context full" signal. **(4) One window per app:** `DocTaskManager.getContextTokens` (main/index.ts) now returns `effectiveContextWindow(activeRuntime, settings)` (fallback override-aware) instead of bare `settings.contextTokens` — doc tasks no longer budget against a different context size than chat/RAG. **(5) User context size (the requested option):** new `AppSettings.contextTokensOverride` (**data contract**: nullable number; null = automatic; clamped [2048, `MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS_OVERRIDE` 32768], junk rejected — the null default defeats the generic type check in `updateSettings`); chat launch (`registerModelIpc`) = `override ?? (manifest.recommendedContextTokens || settings.contextTokens)` — previously the manifest ALWAYS won, so the `chat.truncated.hint` copy pointed at a control that didn't exist and a setting that had no effect; AI Model screen gains the "Context size" card (`CONTEXT_SIZE_PRESETS` 4k/8k/16k/32k + Automatic, next-start semantics + restart note, `models.context.*` en+de); Settings→Workspace shows "Automatic (model default)" instead of the unused number; `effectiveContextWindow`'s no-runtime fallback is override-aware. **Answered for the report:** context IS per conversation (nothing accumulates across chats); a 5-page PDF genuinely saturates a 4096-token window (whole-doc budget ≈ (window−reserve)÷1.5 — §15.1); the picker is the remedy. **Verification:** `npm test` **3286 passed / 44 skipped** (+7: `settings-context-override.test.ts` new; chat.test.ts +3 — fast-cap unflagged, prompt-usage report, override fallback precedence; rag.test.ts +2 — grounded usage > resting read, grounded truncated stamp + clean-run unflagged); typecheck clean. **Next actions:** consider surfacing the live doc-turn usage in the resting state too (a "your attached document uses ~N% per question" hint on documents conversations), and a "continue this reply" affordance on truncated turns (still open from §15.7)._

_2026-07-04 — **Skills audit 2 CLOSE-OUT: audit paper folded into architecture.md §41; wave ready to merge — branch `fix/skills2-close` (off `fix/skills2-t2`).**
The doc-lifecycle close for the 2026-07-03 audit wave (the `fix/skills-close` precedent). **(1) Design record:** `docs/skills-audit-2026-07-03.md` is condensed into **architecture.md §41** ("Skills & Tools audit 2 (2026-07-03) — remediation wave close-out") — the audit's held assessment, the three finding clusters (honesty composition / invented-figure classes / finish-the-inversion), a nine-row per-phase disposition table (R7…T2 with code homes), the wave's six recorded decisions (SKA-20/26/34/43/44/45), the posture paragraph, and a **§-anchor legend** with one row per `SKA-1`…`SKA-45` plus the section anchors (§1–§9) — so the ~38 source files + tests + docs citing `SKA-N` / `skills-audit-2026-07-03 §N.M` stay resolvable with zero comment churn. The §39 A4 paragraph's "to fold into this record's successor" note now points at §41. **(2) Paper retired:** the working paper is DELETED (full original in git history, last at `fix/skills2-t2`); the only kept path-reference is §41's own "was deleted" note (BUILD_STATE history entries stay as history). **(3) Stranded-commit reconcile: none needed** — verified all nine phase branches are ancestors of `fix/skills2-t2` (clean linear stack), unlike the previous wave's `42a4eb9` case. Docs-only commit (no code/test change; suite green at T2's 3272/44). **Next action: merge `fix/skills2-close` into `master`** (master has exactly one commit the stack lacks — `c296803`, downloads size-cap; expect a trivial BUILD_STATE.md both-added conflict), run the full suite + typecheck on the merge result, then delete the merged `fix/skills2-*` (and the already-merged wave-1 `fix/skills-*`) branches._

_2026-07-04 — **Skills audit 2 T2: eval & test-infra sweep + ledger close (FINAL implementation phase) — branch `fix/skills2-t2` (off `fix/skills2-r9`), UNMERGED.**
Ninth and last implementation phase of the 2026-07-03 audit (`docs/skills-audit-2026-07-03.md` §5/§7 T2) — **TEST-INFRA ONLY: zero production-source changes, no extractor-version bump** (the one sanctioned rider, the SKA-43 cache, was weighed and DECLINED — decision below); the T1 snapshot diff is **additive-only** (+2 new fixture keys, 54 inserted lines, 0 deletions — verifies the guard accepts new corpus keys without a bump). **Seven work items, every new test teeth-checked RED against its target (14 checks run → restored green):** (1) **Snapshot-guard self-checks** (the §5 closing bullet): the default (non-UPDATE) path now RECOMPUTES `sha256(stableStringify(prev.output))` against each committed `hash` (a hand-edited hash field fails the suite) and pins the snapshot's recorded bank/invoice extractor versions to the live constants (a bump-without-regenerate fails loudly even when no fixture output moved); the same-commit input-edit exemption is RECORDED as accepted — inherent to corpus upkeep, and an extractor change smuggled through it would need a visible fixture edit in the same diff (test-file comment + known-limitations). (2) **Controller edges** (the two untested `finish()` paths): a runner whose promise REJECTS → the `.catch` → `finish({ok:false})` → 'failed' with the fixed content-free copy (the thrown text never crosses); a runner that throws AFTER abort (signal aborted, no `cancelled` flag) → the `signal.aborted` fallback → 'cancelled', never a red "failed". (3) **persist-failure-keeps-old under `replaceExisting`, BOTH domains**: an injected failure on the fresh CHILD-row insert (after the delete AND the new parent row — the deepest partial state the rollback must unwind) rolls back to the intact PRIOR statement/invoice — same id, rows readable, still `latest*Id`, run row terminal 'failed' (the load-bearing "a failure rolls back to the old" claim in run.ts, previously untested). (4) **Two-conversations-same-document through the real `startSkillRun` IPC**: the controller keys on `documentId` conversation-independently — a second conversation resolving to the SAME document is refused busy (carrying the running handle for re-adopt; recovery-after-clear also pinned), while DIFFERENT documents from different conversations run concurrently. (5) **Corpus additions** (the two §5 fixtures earlier phases didn't land): `bank-at-ocr-dropped-row` — a `31.02.2026` invalid-date money-bearing Rücklastschrift row is dropped AND counted (`droppedRowCount` 1 via the post-R7 LEADING_DATE_SHAPE + hasMoneyToken rule) — and `bank-de-contradicted-closing` — a printed Endsaldo the rows refute (D56 'contradicted', dropped 0). Both snapshot through the production extractor as NEW entries (no bump) and run END-TO-END through the analysis handler: the U1 `countPartial`/`countContradicted` headlines on the template path, and the W6 postscript echo suppression + 'NOT verified as the whole statement' data-block verdict on the contradicted statement's grounded-data path — all with production-reachable questions (`applies()` asserted in-test). (6) **SEC-1 end-to-end at the chat surface** + the W6 IPC rider: an ENABLED user-imported `kind:'tool'` skill declaring `analysis: whole-doc` over a matching fully-chunked statement takes plain RELEVANCE through the real `askDocuments` — no analysis engine, no Tier-2 tool run, no `skill_runs` row, no data block in the model prompt (the parser's `analysisIgnoredForTool` strip pinned as the first of the three layered gates); and the W6 mixed-currency grounded-data IPC pin — the PERSISTED turn carries NO cashflow echo (summing EUR+USD into one net would be an invented figure). (7) **Reachability repair + sweep** (SKA-7's test half): `skills-analysis-bank.test.ts` :743 ("what was my biggest payment?" — pre-W7 that string failed `applies()` and the test pinned a production-UNREACHABLE path by calling `run()` directly) now ASSERTS `applies()` before `run()`, so a future vocabulary regression reds the test instead of silently re-pinning an unreachable path; a T2 sweep of EVERY question the bank + invoice analysis suites drive through `run()` found ALL pass `applies()` — :743 was the only historic bypass (the whole-doc/redaction suites assert their `applies()`/`intends()` gates explicitly already). **Decisions:** **SKA-43 → ACCEPTED PERF RESIDUAL, no cache** — the double per-needle-turn document scan is milliseconds of in-memory string work against the model call that follows (seconds–minutes on CPU); a memoized per-document token total would need invalidation at every chunk-mutation site (re-index, purge, direct `chunks` UPDATEs — a pattern that exists) and a STALE total silently mis-sizes the needle downgrade + compare split: correctness-adjacent risk for an imperceptible win (§3.3 stamp + known-limitations; revisit only if profiling shows the scan hot). **SKA-45's `buildSkillFence` O(n²) loop → documented residual** (bounded by the 64 KiB body cap; ~100–300 ms hostile worst case, once per turn). **Ledger: ALL 45 SKA items are now dispositioned** (fixed-in-phase or documented-residual + known-limitations entry); the §5 testing-audit gap bullets are annotated closed in the report (T2/U6/R9 attribution per item) and §7's T2 spec is stamped DONE. Also repaired: R9's known-limitations edit had accidentally swallowed the T1 bullet's headline line — restored. **Suite: 3272 pass / 44 skip (+17 net vs R9's 3255), typecheck green; the opt-in real-model smoke ran once as the WAVE'S final sanity pass (SKILLS_SMOKE_MODEL=D:/models/chat/qwen3.5-4b-ud-q4kxl.gguf): 3/3 green (bank grounded-data narration + echo, invoice narration + echo, minutes whole-doc coverage) in ~354 s.** Adversarial pass over the new tests themselves: all assert BEHAVIOR (terminal states + fixed copy, DB rows via SQL, persisted IPC content, headlines, routes), none silences without a visible diff — the two injection tests fail VISIBLY if their poisoned SQL string ever drifts (the second extraction would then SUCCEED and the `ok:false` assert reds), and the corpus e2e tests red if the fixtures are weakened (the snapshot pins the fixture inputs). **Data contract: none** (no schema/IPC/source change anywhere). Docs: audit report §3.3 SKA-43 + §3.4 SKA-45 + §5 annotations + §7 T2 stamp; known-limitations.md T2 bullet (SKA-43/SKA-45 residuals + the snapshot-guard acceptance). **THE WAVE IS IMPLEMENTATION-COMPLETE** (R7 → R8 → W6 → W7 → A4 → U6 → U7 → R9 → T2, all on stacked UNMERGED `fix/skills2-*` branches). **The remaining step is the CLOSE-OUT session** (the doc-lifecycle rule / `fix/skills-close` precedent): fold `docs/skills-audit-2026-07-03.md` into an architecture.md design record — §39's successor — with a §-anchor legend keeping the `SKA-N` / `audit §N.M` citations resolvable, retire the working paper, and reconcile any stranded commits._

_2026-07-04 — **Skills audit 2 R9: run-seam edge hardening (SKA-24/26/27/28/44) — branch `fix/skills2-r9` (off `fix/skills2-u7`), UNMERGED.**
Eighth phase of the 2026-07-03 audit (`docs/skills-audit-2026-07-03.md` §3.3/§3.4/§7 R9) — **doc-lock + the A1 run-seam tails + two decide-and-record items (both became CHANGES)**; NO extractor-version bump, NO T1 snapshot interaction (verified: the categorizer rule table is outside the extraction output; `extractor-realworld` green, snapshot byte-untouched). **Five fixes + one review rider.** (1) **SKA-24** — Cancel now REACHES a run parked on the document lock: `withDocumentLock(id, fn, signal?)` is abort-aware — a waiter PARKED behind another lane (a run queued behind a long categorize holding the lock for minutes of LLM batches) rejects with `AbortError` on abort instead of a dead "running" spinner + busy refusals until the other lane finishes. **The chain invariant the abort path preserves:** the waiter's tail (`wait.then(() => mine)`) is PUBLISHED into `chainTails` BEFORE it parks, so the abort path resolves `mine` (fn will never run — nothing else would release it) and prunes the entry once the chain drains — pinned by the abort-a-parked-waiter + THIRD-caller-acquires test. Contract edges: the re-entrant inline path precedes the signal check (a nested acquire never rejects); an already-aborted caller at a FREE lock still runs fn so the seam records its honest 'cancelled' run row (pre-R9 tested contract, pinned); a parked-aborted run creates NO `skill_runs` row at all (prepare never ran — pinned as total-row-count). Threaded: every seam's `deps.signal` (bank+invoice extract/validate/categorize/summarize/export), the analysis handlers' turn signal (`ctx.signal` — a Stop while parked rejects into `withChatStream`, which maps an aborted rejection to the calm empty-done), the categorize doctask's task signal (manager already maps AbortError → 'cancelled'). The controller needs NO change: its existing `.catch` + `signal.aborted` fallback flips the run to 'cancelled'. (2) **SKA-27** — `runDomainFileExport`'s post-prepare tail is B4-guarded (`finishTail`: every terminal write in try/catch + ONE retry for the transient-lock class): a `finishRun` throw after the minutes-open save dialog no longer strands the row at 'started' NOR reports "failed. Nothing was changed." after the file WAS written — past the write, the outcome reports what happened to the FILE (bookkeeping failure → local log only, `ok:true` stands); 'done' is stamped at WRITE-time `now()`, not the pre-dialog prepare `completedAt` (run history no longer timestamps an export minutes early). (3) **SKA-28** — the two downstream seams that held NO outer lock (`runCashflowSummary`, `runDomainFileExport`; validate/categorize already wrapped) now hold ONE re-entrant per-document lock across prepare (incl. the R3 staleness re-extract) + row load + serialization, closing the TOCTOU where a competing `replaceExisting` extract interleaved between the self-locked re-extract's RELEASE and the unlocked load (empty CSV, "saved 0 rows" — the barrier-forced test reproduced it deterministically red pre-fix). **Lock scope:** the export's hold RELEASES BEFORE the save dialog (the serialized `{text,rowCount}` is materialized inside the hold; a minutes-open dialog must never block the categorize doctask / chat analysis on that document) — pinned by an `activeDocumentLockCount()===0`-during-dialog probe. Synergy: a parked export/summarize is now cancellable (SKA-24). (4) **SKA-26 DECISION: FLIP to `!==`** (both domains) — extractor-version DOWNGRADE now re-extracts: `isBankStatementStale`/`isInvoiceStale` treat `v !== CURRENT` (not `<`) as stale. Reasoning: the app runs FROM the portable drive so it roams WITH the workspace — a mismatch means a deliberate ROLLBACK (where the newer extractor IS the suspected bug; serving its rows as fresh was exactly backwards) or a second install; deterministic extractors make `!==` loop-free (re-extract stamps CURRENT). **Accepted cost (the trade-off, recorded in known-limitations):** a workspace ALTERNATED between two app versions re-extracts on every switch, and each `replaceExisting` re-extract DROPS the persisted per-row categories — accepted because the rows changed with the parser, so recomputing is the honest move (the existing `replaceExisting` doctrine), and the alternating-installs case is rare vs the silently-wrong-figures rollback case. `v = CURRENT+1` stale pins added (bank + invoice; only older-is-stale was pinned before). (5) **SKA-44 DECISION: DEMOTE** — the EN `transfer` categorizer rule is `confident: false` like R3's `sepa`/`überweisung`: "TRANSFER TO NETFLIX…" is a Netflix charge, not a 'Transfer' — the word names the RAILS, so the row now reaches the 15-category LLM (pinned end-to-end: in the model batch, takes the richer bucket) while `categorizeRow` keeps the deterministic offline 'Transfer' fallback (pinned); the old "stays confident" test was the one pin of the pre-R9 behaviour and was rewritten. NO extractor bump (R3 precedent — categories are not extraction output). **Tests (+10 net vs U7's 3245, 3255 pass / 44 skip + typecheck green):** concurrency (abort-a-parked-waiter + third-caller + no-leak, already-aborted-free-lock contract, controller end-to-end cancel-while-parked [bounded poll, no hop-count dependence], TOCTOU export + summarize twins [competing replace-delete parked on the lock → never an empty file / 0-count]), skills-run (injected once-throwing `UPDATE skill_runs` seam → ok:true + row 'done', write-time timestamp pin, lock-released-before-dialog probe, bank downgrade pin), skills-invoice (invoice downgrade pin), categorizer ×3 (SKA-44). **Teeth-checked: every fix reverted → red** (SKA-24 signal-neutered → 3 red [+ documented count-leak cascade]; SKA-28 export-lock removed → "saved 0 rows" reproduced; summarize-lock removed → red; SKA-27 unguarded+pre-dialog-stamp → 2 red; SKA-26 `!==`→`<` → 2 red; SKA-44 → 3 red; both review riders → red) → restored green; the teeth lens ALSO ran all 13 new/changed assertions against a pre-fix worktree — all red. **Adversarial 3-lens review (lock-ordering/abort-path, seam-lifecycle/contracts, test-teeth/flakiness — parallel agents, every finding independently verified, the teeth lens empirically via a detached pre-fix worktree + 5× flake loop):** no deadlock/double-grant/leak/stranded-row/unhandled-rejection found (the abort path's microtask walks, all four rejection consumers, and the no-new-cycle lock ordering each verified clean); **1 Medium product rider fixed:** `runDocumentRedaction` — the OTHER dialog-shaped seam — carried the exact SKA-27 class (unguarded post-write 'done' → outer B4 catch → 'failed' + "Nothing was changed." after the redacted copy WAS written) → same guarded-retry treatment + test; **2 Medium test hardenings fixed:** nothing pinned the lock-releases-before-dialog scope (a hold-across-dialog variant passed the whole suite → the `activeDocumentLockCount()` dialog probe added, teeth-checked) and a red assertion before a barrier release leaked the module-global chain into later tests' count assertions (empirically confirmed cascade → try/finally barrier releases); 2 Low (run-row pin widened to total-count; try/finally on the db.prepare monkey-patch restore). Review residuals accepted-not-fixed: the aborted-waiter's settled-tail prune window can reject an already-aborted FREE-lock caller for ~1 microtask (unreachable with the callers' fresh-per-run signals; a synchronous prune would be wrong); `readOutput` inside the lock is pre-`finishTail` (unreachable — cast+destructure of schema-validated output). **Data contract: none** (no schema/IPC change; `withDocumentLock`'s third param is optional/additive). Docs: doc-lock.ts header (abort-aware §), architecture.md §9 PC-1 paragraph + §13 PC-1 bullet (abort-aware acquisition; summarize/export outer holds; lock scope excludes the dialog; the stale "export paths need not lock" claim corrected), run.ts `reExtract` rationale + both extractor-version constant docstrings (`!==` semantics), known-limitations.md R9 bullet (all five + the SKA-26 category-loss trade + SKA-24/27 residuals + the redaction rider), audit §3.3 SKA-24/26/27/28 + §3.4 SKA-44 stamped fixed-in-R9 + §7 R9 title DONE. Branch left UNMERGED; next per audit §8: T2 (eval & test-infra sweep — last phase)._

_2026-07-04 — **Skills audit 2 U7: package lifecycle hardening (SKA-15/16/30-36/42 + SKA-45 riders) — branch `fix/skills2-u7` (off `fix/skills2-u6`), UNMERGED.**
Seventh phase of the 2026-07-03 audit (`docs/skills-audit-2026-07-03.md` §3.2/§3.3/§7 U7) — **skill packages, manifest parsing, installer/registry, the fence-trim minimum, SkillsTab error surfacing**; NO extractor/routing/run-store change, no T1 snapshot interaction. SKA-31 landed BEFORE SKA-32 per the phase ordering constraint (surfacing first would have piped attacker frontmatter into app.log/IPC — the §22-M1 leak). **Ten fixes + two riders.** (1) **SKA-15** (Medium) — the fence-trim "guaranteed minimum" was the bare `#` heading: `buildSkillFence` guarantees ONLY `paragraphs[0]`, and U1's layout kept the honesty rules at P2, so a budget-squeezed turn shipped an intro PROMISING rules with none delivered (the decapitation class U1 claimed closed), and the parity test pinned the intro at `paras[1]` claiming it survives. Fixed **DATA-ONLY** (the prescribed option): all 8 bundled SKILL.md bodies merge heading + intro + bullets into ONE paragraph (the builder — load-bearing and tested — is untouched); the parity test re-pointed (paras[0] starts `#` ∧ "lead and always apply" ∧ ≥3 bullets) and now trims each REAL body through `buildSkillFence` at a minimum-sized budget asserting the bullets survive (the audit acceptance); the `logSkillFenceReduction` "paragraph count" docstring ghost corrected (rider). Keywords/frontmatter untouched ⇒ W5 parity + suggestion behaviour byte-identical. (2) **SKA-16** (Medium) — one unreadable SKILL.md (e.g. a DIRECTORY of that name from hand-unpacking) threw EISDIR out of `parseSkillManifestFromDir` through `discoverSkillsInDir`, and every caller swallowed it: ALL reconciliation dead for the session, imports erroring installed-but-rowless. Fix at BOTH layers: manifest.ts stats SKILL.md `throwIfNoEntry:false` + requires `isFile()` (non-file ⇒ the friendly "not found" result), registry.ts guards every per-folder read (stat + parse try/catch → structural code + `continue`). (3) **SKA-30** — `createCaseFoldGuard`: the S-2 duplicate-path check is CASE-FOLDED (lowercased file paths + directory PREFIXES, refusing the file-vs-dir casing merge) and shared by `stageZip` AND `stageFolder` — `SKILL.md`+`skill.md` polyglot zips refuse instead of last-writer-winning on NTFS/exFAT while installing different instructions per OS; ASCII-fold residual documented. (4) **SKA-31** — YAML parse errors: fixed structural message + validated NUMERIC `err.linePos` line/column only, never `String(err)` (the yaml pretty error quotes the offending source line); canary sentinel test (parse-error path — the existing sentinel covered permission notes only). (5) **SKA-32** — discovery errors now REACH someone: structured `errorCodes` ('invalidFolderName'|'unreadable'|'invalidManifest'|'duplicateId') parallel the error strings; startup logs count + deduped codes; the registry keeps `reconcileStatus()` and the NEW additive `skills:reconcileStatus` IPC drives a Settings → Skills "N skill folders could not be read" notice (en+de, count-pluralized). Content nuance enforced: an INVALID folder name is arbitrary user text — omitted from the line, code-only; the human-readable strings never leave the process. (6) **SKA-33** — a failed IMPORT now shows the precise localized reason: NEW `renderer/lib/skillImportI18n.ts` maps the wrapped Electron IPC message back through the code table (longest-first `includes`; EN-catalog ≡ `SKILL_IMPORT_ERRORS` byte-pinned by test), as preview always did. (7) **SKA-34 DECISION: option (b)** — export mirrors IMPORT's acceptance (everything allowed under the skill dir up to the import depth cap, minus the root manifest.json cache/dot-entries/symlinks; `EXPORT_SUBDIRS` deleted) — option (a) would refuse packages every prior version accepted; plus the review-driven symmetric half: import now SKIPS dot-named entries (zip + folder — a `.git`-carrying folder even imports cleanly now), so `export(import(pkg)) == pkg` holds EXACTLY (round-trip fidelity test: non-canonical `notes/usage.md` + root `data.csv` byte-identical through import→export→re-import on a fresh machine). (8) **SKA-35** — preview notes emit stable CODES + APP-FIXED params (a `NoteCollector` keeps `notes`/`noteCodes` index-parallel through validation/parse/preview; `formatSkillNote` is the single string source); the `localized.<key>` family DROPS the attacker-chosen locale key; NEW `localizedTooMany` over-cap note; renderer localizes via `IMPORT_NOTE_KEY` (en+de, 20 keys ×2) with structural fallback. (9) **SKA-36** — `sweepStaleSkillTempDirs` at reconcile removes crash-leftover `.skill-import-*`/`.skill-backup-*` dirs, AGE-GATED > 1 h by mtime (reconcile runs DURING importSkill and a concurrent instance could own a fresh dir — startup-only would not be a sufficient guard). (10) **SKA-42** — document-redaction/SKILL.md names BOTH button labels ("**Redact personal data** / **Personenbezogene Daten schwärzen**", label follows the app language), exactly matching `chat.skill.tool.redactDocument`. **Riders (SKA-45):** the stale S13a-era autoFire comment refreshed to U4-era wording; Unicode bidi direction controls (U+202A–202E, U+2066–2069) rejected in displayed frontmatter strings (title/description/author — error; localized overrides — ignored-with-note). **Data contract (additive):** `SkillParseResult.noteCodes?`/`SkillManifestValidation.noteCodes?` (`SkillNoteRef {code, params}`), `SkillPreview.noteCodes?`, `DiscoveryResult/ReconcileResult.errorCodes`, `SkillRegistry.reconcileStatus()`, the `skills:reconcileStatus` IPC + `SkillReconcileStatus`. **Tests (+44 net vs U6's 3201, 3245 pass / 44 skip + typecheck green):** parity/fence (real-body trim at rules-only budget ×8, one-paragraph shape ×8), registry (dir-SKILL.md survives discovery+reconcile, error codes parallel, reconcileStatus lifecycle, sweep age-gate incl. fresh-dir spare, dot-dir invisibility), installer (case-folded dup + file-vs-dir merge + no-over-reject, dot-entry skip zip+folder, round-trip fidelity + litter exclusion), manifest (YAML canary, note-code parallelism + formatSkillNote round-trip, locale-key canary, over-cap note, bidi title/localized/language), renderer (SKA-32 banner, SKA-33 precise + generic-fallback toasts, SKA-35 coded-note localization), IPC (reconcileStatus counts/codes + sentinel-free + phantom-notice refresh after import), skillImportI18n unit (EN≡constants parity both directions, wrapped-message mapping, substring pair, dead-key sweep). **Teeth-checked: ALL TEN fixes individually reverted → red (2/1/2/1/2/3/1/5/1/2 tests) → restored green.** **Adversarial 3-lens review (installer/zip, parser/registry/privacy, renderer/IPC/prompt — parallel agents, every finding independently verified) → 4 Low + 2 Info, ALL addressed:** (a) rename PRESERVES mtime, so a fresh `.skill-backup-*` inherited the old install's stale mtime and a CONCURRENT second instance's sweep could delete a live backup mid-import (no `requestSingleInstanceLock` exists), breaking the M1 restore → the backup dir's mtime is TOUCHED after the rename; (b) `reconcileStatus` went stale after import/delete (the installer reconciles via the module function, not the handle) — a user FIXING a broken drop-in by importing kept a phantom notice until restart → the import/delete IPC handlers refresh via `ctx.skills.reconcile()` (+ the false registry comment corrected, + IPC test); (c) the import handler's catch-all rethrew `invalidManifest` for UNEXPECTED throws (ENOSPC, AV lock) which SKA-33 would now surface as confident WRONG copy → replaced with a fixed content-free UNMAPPED message so the generic toast holds (+ renderer test); (d) `language` was the one DISPLAYED frontmatter string the bidi rider missed → same lenient gate (+ newline), note + default 'en'; (e) dot-entry import/export asymmetry (a `.hidden.md` installed then vanished from re-export — the exact SKA-34 class) → import skips dot entries, symmetric (see 7); (f) a < 1 h crash leftover surfaced as a phantom SKA-32 folder error → discovery skips dot-named dirs outright. Also corrected: the "re-derived cache" export comment (nothing ever writes manifest.json — it is the optional cache SKILL.md outranks). **Residuals documented (known-limitations):** ASCII-only case folding; export of a hand-made DROP-IN can pack files a re-import refuses (over-cap sizes, case-collisions born on a case-sensitive FS) — export deliberately applies no caps to the user's own files; the `buildSkillFence` O(n²) growth micro stays open (T2/R9 candidate); the IMPORT_ERROR_KEY `locked` entry is unreachable on the import path (requireUnlocked throws its own localized string — dead mapping, harmless). Docs: known-limitations U7 bullet + U1 fence-trim annotation (audit §4.3), security-model.md (SKA-30 case-fold on the S-2 bullet, SKA-34 export policy, SKA-31/32/35 content-free notes, backup mtime touch), audit §3.2/§3.3 stamped fixed-in-U7 (all ten) + §3.4 SKA-45 riders + §4 items 3/4/6/8 + §5 lifecycle gaps + §7 U7 title. Branch left UNMERGED; next per audit §8: R9 (run-seam edge hardening) → T2._

_2026-07-04 — **Skills audit 2 U6: renderer run lifecycle (SKA-6/17/18/25/29/37/38/39/40/41) — branch `fix/skills2-u6` (off `fix/skills2-a4`), UNMERGED.**
Sixth phase of the 2026-07-03 audit (`docs/skills-audit-2026-07-03.md` §3.1/§3.2/§3.3/§7 U6). **RENDERER STORE + a small additive IPC surface** — NO extractor/routing change (analysis/, tools/ untouched). **Core problem (SKA-6, High):** after A2 made runs per-document concurrent MAIN-side, the renderer kept a SINGLE module-level `active` run and ChatScreen rendered that one app-wide run's bar in EVERY conversation — a second run silently abandoned the first, a conversation switch could categorize the WRONG document, and a routed answer could be dismissed from the wrong chat. **Ten fixes.** (1) **SKA-6** — `renderer/lib/skillruns.ts` re-architected into a **multi-run store keyed by `runHandle`** (each entry `{run, conversationId, documentId}`, all live handles polled independently); ChatScreen derives the active conversation's run via `pickConversationRun(skillRuns, activeId)` and renders the busy/result bar ONLY when `conversationId === activeId`, with a quiet **"working in another chat"** chip (`hasRunningRunElsewhere`) for runs elsewhere (new en+de strings, du-form). The post-extract **Categorize** offer is hidden when its remembered document left the current scope (`categorizeTargetInScope`), never relying on main's single-doc fallback. `useSyncExternalStore` snapshot is referentially stable. (2) **SKA-17** — reload re-attach: NEW `listSkillRuns` IPC (returns `SkillRunState[]`, running AND terminal-unacknowledged); the store's `adoptSkillRuns()` re-adopts on a fresh mount and the ChatScreen mount effect re-lands on the run's conversation (`getReattachConversationId`); `conversationId`/`documentId` threaded ADDITIVELY onto the content-free `SkillRunState` (+ `StartRunArgs`) so a reload can re-gate + re-pin; a busy refusal carries `runningHandle` (`StartSkillRunResult`) as a single-handle re-attach fallback (`adoptHandle`); the controller TTL-sweeps never-acknowledged terminal runs (30 min) on `start()`/`list()`. (3) **SKA-18** — `ChatScreen.ensureConversation` DELETES the `'new'` keys from `skillByConv`/`keepByConv` after re-keying, so a kept pick doesn't resurrect on a later empty composer. (4) **SKA-29** (main-side half of SKA-6) — `startSkillRun` HARD-REFUSES `documentOutOfScope` for a CONFIRM-GATED tool whose requested id is out of scope even at a single-doc scope (`docIds.length > 1 || confirmGated`); read-only tools keep the tested single-doc fallback. (5) **SKA-25** — the cancel/clear IPC require a NON-EMPTY handle (the no-arg cancel-all/clear-all is now internal/test-only); `requireUnlocked` added to get/list/cancel/clear. (6) **SKA-39/40** — store robustness: shallow-compare (`state`/`count`/`resultKind`/`progress`/`errorCode`) before notifying so a 400 ms no-op poll no longer re-renders ChatScreen; tolerate `MAX_POLL_FAILURES` (3) consecutive poll errors then keep a labelled *"state unknown"* row (dismissable) instead of dropping a live run; `done/total` progress rendered in the busy row (aria-hidden, review-hardened). (7) **SKA-37** — `Transcript.tsx` `lastAssistantId` is the LAST message only when it is the assistant turn, so a trailing unanswered USER turn suppresses the undo + Try again (the failed-generation retry stays covered by the error banner). (8) **SKA-38** — `chat.ts rowToMessage` returns the RAW persisted `messages.skill_id` (present even when deleted) + the JOIN `skillTitle` (null when deleted) + `autoFired` following the stamp; `Transcript.tsx` keys the glyph+undo off `m.skillId` and labels a gone-skill turn **"(removed skill)"** (`chat.skill.removed`, en+de) — a DELETED skill now keeps its provenance, consistent with a disabled one. (9) **SKA-41** — one always-mounted `role="status" aria-live="polite"` container (`.skill-run-live`); the run row renders inside it, the offer outside (minimal `styles.css` for `.skill-run-live`/`.skill-run-progress`/`.skill-run-elsewhere`). **The routed-run relay invariants survive the rewrite:** C1 (answer lands only in the launching conversation — the bar is conversation-gated), C2 (routed under the RUN's skill), ux-6 (pinned to the run's document, resolved from the store entry BEFORE acknowledge). **Data-contract additions (ADDITIVE):**
